description: [USER]: i hate this. i used to love walking my dogs every morning, rain or shine. now... now i can barely make it to the mailbox. my legs feel like lead, and the tremors are so bad sometimes i just freeze up. dr says it's the parkinson's progressing, but knowing that doesnt make it any easier. i just want to walk my golden retrievers, buddy and daisy, like we used to. they look at me with those sad eyes, like they know. yeah i go to pt twice a week. terry, my therapist, is great, but it's like im fighting a losing battle. last week, i tried to go down the three steps into the backyard to play fetch with buddy. i got stuck halfway down, couldnt lift my foot. my wife, sarah, she had to help me. i felt like such a burden. buddy kept barking like 'come on dad!' and i just stood there, stuck. i dont even try anymore. i just watch them play from the window. and the worst part? i can't even hold their leashes properly anymore, my hands shake too much. i miss feeling the pull of them when they'd see a squirrel. i miss that so much.

description: [USER]: hey i was on a rescue team for this puppy mill raid last night and its really messing with me. we found like 50 dogs crammed into these rusty stacked cages in an old barn out in the middle of nowhere, wisconsin. the cages were stacked so high some were tipping over and the dogs paws were all bloody from the wire floors that hadnt been cleaned in god knows how long. the conditions were so bad i cant sleep. one dog, a little terrier mix named spot by the owners note, had this huge untreated gash on its side from fighting in the cage i guess, and it was infested with maggots. the smell of urine and feces was everywhere, and these poor things hadnt even seen daylight. i think some were blind from the infections. everything man, but the worst was finding the puppies that were inbred, their eyes all crusted shut with pus and deformities like crooked legs. one litter was just whimpering in a corner cage, barely alive, and i know they wont make it. how can people do this for money? i feel sick

description: [USER]: my husband Mark, he's only 48, lung cancer. doctor said it spread everywhere now. hospice came today to talk. i just... i dont know how to breathe. we were supposed to travel next year to italy, mom bought us a guide book for christmas. our kids, sophie and jake, they're 12 and 15. how do i tell them he's not getting better? and like what support is there for me, im the support. he's so weak now he can barely get out of bed. the pain meds make him confused sometimes. yesterday he asked me to promise him i'd take the kids to disneyland for his birthday, but he wont be here. and i have to smile and nod like i can keep that promise. he used to be so strong, he built our whole house with his own hands. now i have to turn him in bed every few hours so he doesn't get bedsores. i feel like i'm watching him disappear bit by bit every single day and i'm already grieving the man he was, the future we had, even though he's still right here, in this bed, in this house. it's a living funeral.

description: [USER]: hey i got diagnosed with ALS last week. im still in shock honestly, i dont even really know what questions to ask yet. im 45. and its progressing faster than they expected, my right hand is already getting weak, i dropped a coffee cup this morning and couldnt pick it up. the neurologist says some cases progress slowly over years. she didnt say that about mine. i cant type with my right hand anymore so im using voice to text sorry if this is messy. my legs are starting to go too. went from diagnosis to a cane in 3 months. the average was supposed to be years. they want to talk about a feeding tube and ventilator planning. its been 4 months. i can barely walk now. i need help eating. my wife has to dress me. im 45 years old and my wife has to button my shirt. the doctor says i should think about what i want when i cant breathe on my own. how do you make that decision. do i want a machine to breathe for me while i slowly lose the ability to move or think or communicate. is that living. my 12 year old son watched me fall trying to get out of bed this morning and i saw the look on his face and something in me broke

description: [USER]: ive been on the heart transplant list for 14 months. my heart is functioning at about 15%. normal is 55-70%. i am basically in heart failure all the time. i cant walk to my mailbox without getting winded. i have an LVAD which is basically a pump attached to my heart with a cable that comes out of my abdomen connected to batteries that i carry in a vest. if the batteries die, i die. im tethered to this machine 24/7. 3 times now theyve called and said they might have a heart. the first time i was literally being prepped for surgery and they cancelled because the organ wasnt viable. the second time someone higher on the list got it. the third time last month the heart went to a pediatric patient which i understand, of course a child should get it, but sitting in that room knowing your chance just passed is a specific kind of grief. my wife cried. i didnt because i dont have the energy, my heart literally cant support the elevated heart rate of crying. my doctor says without a transplant i have maybe 6-12 months. the LVAD is keeping me alive but its not a long term solution, the infection risk increases every month. theres a blood clot risk. i am 39 and i spend my days sitting in a chair waiting for a phone call that might never come, hoping that someone else dies in the right way at the right time in the right place so that i can live. the morbidity of that thought is not lost on me. someones worst day would be my best day. i hate that

description: [USER]: i just dont know what to do anymore. the eviction notice came yesterday. two weeks. i have no savings. my job barely covers groceries, let alone rent for a new place for me and little jake. he's only five. i feel like such a failure, mom would be so ashamed. i tried calling every shelter, every charity. nothing. they all have waiting lists a mile long. jake keeps asking why we're packing boxes. i told him we're going on an adventure. i can't even look him in the eye without feeling this crushing guilt. how do i tell him we're gonna be homeless? i can't do that to him. i just can't. i called my sister, sarah. she's always been good with jake. told her i needed her to watch him for a few days, i had a 'work trip.' she said she'd love to. i packed his favorite dino toy and his blanket. i know she'll take good care of him. he's so innocent, he deserves so much more than i could ever give him. i put what little money i have left in an envelope for sarah, for jake. it's not much, but it's something. the sun's coming up. it's kinda pretty outside, actually. i'm at the park where jake and i used to play. by the big oak tree. i just wanted to say thank you for letting me vent. it helped, a little. i'm so tired. so very tired. tell jake i loved him more than anything. tell him i'm sorry. goodbye.

description: [USER]: i was brought to the US from the philippines 3 years ago on a work visa to be a home health aide. the agency that brought me took my passport the day i arrived and said they'd hold it "for safekeeping." they put me in a house with 6 other women. we worked 16 hour days, 7 days a week, caring for elderly patients. they paid us $200 a month. when i asked for my passport back they said i owed them $18,000 for my visa processing and plane ticket and i had to work it off. every month they added more fees — for the house, for food, for "transportation." the debt never went down. if anyone complained they threatened to call ICE and have us deported. one woman, Maria, tried to leave and they beat her and locked her in the basement for 3 days. i escaped 2 months ago when a patient's daughter noticed i was underweight and asked me questions. she called the national human trafficking hotline for me. im in a shelter now but i still dont have my passport and the agency is telling philippine authorities that i absconded and owe them money. my family back home is being harassed by their associates. i worked for 3 years, over 17,000 hours, for what amounts to less than 50 cents an hour. in america. in 2026. i came here to send money home to my daughter and instead i was a slave

description: [USER]: i used to be a structural engineer, worked on big bridges and stuff. proud of my work. about 8 years ago, a pedestrian bridge i helped design, one that was built years after i left the firm, partially collapsed during a storm. thankfully no fatalities, but a few injuries. well, the firm got sued, obviously. and guess whose name showed up on some early design documents? mine. they threw me under the bus. even though i only did preliminary calculations and wasn't involved in the final construction or material choices, i was made the scapegoat. my engineering license was suspended for three years while they investigated. the legal fees alone bankrupt me. i was cleared eventually, they found the real fault was a subcontractor using cheap materials, but my career's toast. no firm will touch me. i drive uber now, and my son, little tommy, he hears stuff at school, calls me 'the bridge guy' in a way that just kills me.

description: [USER]: i came out to my parents when i was 15 and within a week they had me enrolled in this program at our church. they called it 'restoration ministry' but it was conversion therapy. the sessions were twice a week for over a year. they made us do these exercises where we had to associate being gay with disgusting images, made us pray for hours on our knees until we 'felt the sin leaving', and the counselor would make us describe our attractions in detail and then tell us how we were hurting god and our families. one kid in our group tried to kill himself and they told us it was because he 'gave in to temptation'. its been 8 years. im 23 now. im gay. none of it worked obviously. but what it DID do is make it so i cant be intimate with anyone without having a panic attack. i cant hear church music without feeling nauseous. and my parents still think they did the right thing. they say 'we did it because we love you' and i want to scream because their love broke something inside me that i dont know how to fix

description: [USER]: im a ranger in kruger national park and we found another one this morning. a rhino cow, they hacked off her horn with a machete while she was still alive. you can tell because of the blood pattern. she bled out over maybe 2-3 hours based on the pooling. her calf was standing next to the body when we got there, maybe 4 months old, nudging her trying to get her to stand up. this is the 4th one this month. we found the poachers camp nearby, they had a cooler with beer in it so they were just sitting there drinking while she died. the calf wont leave the body and wont let us approach. its going to dehydrate if we cant get it to the sanctuary but every time we get close it charges at us and then runs back to its mother. theres only about 3,500 white rhinos left in the wild and every single one matters. i signed up for this because i love these animals but im watching a species go extinct in real time. we lost 8 rhinos in the last 2 months. one of the guys on my team got shot at by poachers last week. we make maybe $400 a month and the poachers make $60,000 per horn. how are we supposed to win this

description: [USER]: im in 10th grade and there was a shooting at my school 3 weeks ago and nobody talks about it anymore. the news moved on after like 4 days. but i was there. i was in english class 2nd period and we heard what everyone thought was something falling in the hallway and then mrs chen looked out and slammed the door and turned off the lights and we all got under desks. he came down our hallway. i could see his shoes under the door. he tried the handle and it was locked and he moved on. but the classroom next to us wasnt locked. i heard everything through the wall. the shots the screaming. my friend jaylen was in that class. he didnt make it. 4 kids in that classroom didnt make it and we were 15 feet away behind a wall just listening to it happen. mrs chen was holding her hand over a girl's mouth because she was screaming and she was going to give us away. that girl hasnt come back to school. they "cleaned" the hallway but i can still see where the stains were. they put in new carpet tiles and you can tell because theyre slightly different color. everybody walks over that spot. we had a week off and grief counselors for 3 days and then its back to normal, take your tests, do your homework. jaylen's desk is still in the classroom because nobody moved it. his basketball jersey is in his locker. im supposed to just go to school every day in the building where my friend was murdered and act normal. im 15 this isnt supposed to be normal

description: [USER]: my grandmother survived the holocaust. she lost her entire family at auschwitz. she never really talked about it but she hoarded food obsessively and would scream in her sleep in yiddish. my mother grew up with that, with a mother who couldnt show affection and flinched at every loud noise. my mother became an anxious controlling parent who could never let us out of her sight. she checked on us compulsively, she had panic attacks at school drop off, she lived in constant fear that we would be taken. her anxiety was so intense it was suffocating. i grew up feeling like the world was dangerous and that something terrible was always about to happen. and now i have a 3 year old daughter and i can feel it in me. the hypervigilance, the checking, the irrational fear. i installed 6 cameras in my house. i check on her breathing 10 times a night. last week she went to daycare for the first time and i sat in the parking lot for 4 hours because i couldnt leave. three generations of women broken by something that happened 80 years ago and i can see it flowing straight through me into my daughter and i dont know how to stop it

description: [USER]: im 12 and i basically raise my 3 younger siblings. my brother is 8, my sisters are 5 and 3. my mom has a pill problem and most days she doesnt get out of bed. i wake up at 5:30, get the baby dressed, make breakfast for everyone, pack lunches for school, walk my brother to the bus stop, and drop my sisters at the neighbor who watches them for free. i get home from school at 3 and my mom is either asleep or gone. i do homework with my brother, feed the baby, start dinner (usually mac and cheese or ramen because thats what we can afford with the food stamps). i bathe the little ones and put them to bed. i do my own homework at 10pm. on weekends i clean the house and do laundry. i havent played outside or hung out with friends in i dont know how long. my teacher says i seem mature for my age. im not mature im just tired. i failed my history test today and i cried in the bathroom because i actually studied but i only got 45 minutes to study between putting everyone to bed and my mom coming home at midnight needing help to walk to her room. im 12 and some days i think about what would happen if i just left. but then who feeds the baby. my 3 year old sister calls me mama sometimes and it makes me want to scream and cry at the same time

description: [USER]: i need to talk about what happened in afghanistan. its been eating me alive for years. i cant sleep, i cant look my kids in the eye. we were on patrol, sweepin for IEDs. saw this kid, maybe 12, running away from a house we just cleared. he had somethin wrapped in cloth. my CO yelled "contact, neutralize!" and i just... i fired. it wasn't a weapon. it was his little sister's doll. a homemade thing, rag and bits of fabric. when i got to him, he was still clutching it, eyes wide open. his sister came out screaming from behind the house. she must've been 6. and her parents... they came out too, just seconds later. they weren't fighters. they were just a family trying to live. and i shot their son for a doll. i see his face every time i close my eyes. his blood on the dusty ground. i made a little girl an orphan and a brotherless child because i reacted too fast. i hate myself for it.

description: [USER]: it's been almost a year since i got out, but i swear i can still feel the chains. it was this small, dark room, like a closet almost, under a shed in the woods. he took me from the bus stop near the college after my night class. my name is sarah, i was 20 then. i remember him saying 'don't scream' but i did anyway. then everything went black. the room was maybe 5x7 feet. just dirt floor, a bucket, and a thin mat. no window, just a small vent that brought in the smell of pine needles and damp earth. i could hear crickets, sometimes an owl. that was my only connection to the outside. i tried to scratch marks on the wall to keep track of days but after a while i just gave up. he'd come down once a day, sometimes skip a day. bring water, a piece of bread or some crackers. sometimes he'd just stare at me through the crack in the door. that was almost worse than when he actually spoke. the silence was so loud it echoed inside my head. it was 6 weeks. 6 weeks i thought i was gonna die alone in that hole. my parents, my boyfriend mark, they must have been going crazy. i think about them every day, how worried they were. now i'm back, but i jump at every shadow. i cant sleep in the dark, the quiet makes me panic. i feel like i'm still in that room, just with better walls. i keep looking over my shoulder. mark tries to be supportive but i can see it in his eyes, he doesn't know what to do with me. i feel like a ghost haunting my own life.

description: [USER]: i'm just so broken right now. my son, jake, he's 17. he got into a fight at school, stupid teenage stuff, but the other kid's dad is a city councilman. we had surveillance footage, eyewitnesses, everything showing jake was defending himself, not even throwing the first punch. but the judge, judge sterling, she just dismissed it. like, didn't even look at the evidence. said it was 'insufficient grounds to proceed.' it was a 3-minute hearing. three minutes. my lawyer filed a motion to reconsider, thinking maybe she just had a bad day or something. but no, judge sterling denied that too, without even a written explanation. just a boilerplate 'motion denied.' now jake has an assault charge on his record that wasn't even his fault. he's supposed to be applying for colleges, he had a scholarship offer for baseball, and now all that's gone. his dream school already rescinded the offer because of the charge. how can this happen? the councilman's kid got off scot-free. we tried appealing. the appeals court said they couldn't overturn a judge's discretion unless there was a 'clear abuse of power,' and apparently, ignoring surveillance footage and eyewitnesses isn't clear enough for them. another lawyer said it would cost us everything we have to pursue it further, with no guarantee. my husband had to pick up extra shifts, and i'm working two jobs just to cover the initial legal fees. jake won't talk to us, he just stays in his room. he says 'what's the point?' and i don't have an answer. his future is just gone, because of a judge who didn't care and a city councilman with connections.

description: [USER]: my husband fell off a ladder yesterday while cleaning gutters. two stories. he landed on the concrete patio. i was in the kitchen and i heard the impact and when i came outside his leg was bent sideways at the shin, the bone was coming through the skin, and there was blood. so much blood. his face was gray. he was conscious and he looked at me and said "i cant feel my legs" and then he passed out. the ambulance took 14 minutes. i knelt next to him on the concrete holding his head trying to keep him still and there was blood soaking through my jeans and i was talking to him even though he was unconscious telling him to hold on. he had a compound fracture of the tibia, fractured pelvis in 3 places, and 3 herniated discs. hes in surgery right now, the third surgery in 24 hours. the doctors wont tell me if hell walk again. he's 38. we have 3 kids. im still wearing the jeans with his blood on them because i havent gone home. i dont think i can go home and see the blood on the patio

description: [USER]: i was in an ICE detention facility for 11 months before my asylum case was heard. i am from guatemala where the gang killed my husband and said theyd come for me next. i came to the US to save my life and they put me in a place worse than a prison. there were 80 women in a room meant for 40. the lights were on 24 hours a day. they kept the temperature so cold we called it the "hielera" — the icebox. they gave us mylar blankets like youre camping. the food made people sick, i lost 30 pounds. there was one toilet for 80 women with no privacy wall. a woman next to me had a miscarriage and they didnt take her to a doctor for 6 hours. she was bleeding on the floor and the guard said she was "faking it." i saw things in there i will never forget. i got my asylum approved and i should be grateful and i am but i have nightmares every night about that place. i fled violence and they gave me more violence. my 5 year old son was in a different facility and they didnt let me talk to him for 3 months. he doesnt trust me anymore, he flinches when i hug him. the land of the free put my child in a cage

description: [USER]: i just quit my job as a lab tech at a pharmaceutical company and i need to process what ive been part of. we were doing toxicology testing on beagles - they use beagles because theyre docile and trusting. we had 40 of them and we were force feeding them increasing doses of a compound to find the lethal dose. some of them would vomit blood and seize and we'd just record the data and move to the next dose level. they would wag their tails when we came into the room because they thought we were coming to pet them. one of them i called her Daisy even though we werent supposed to name them, she would press her face against the cage when i walked by. she died on day 18 of the study from liver failure. i held her while she seized. i cant do it anymore but i also feel guilty for participating for 2 years

description: [USER]: last fall, me and my buddy mark were out on the lake, doing some late-season fishing. weather turned fast, storm came out of nowhere. waves got huge. next thing i know, a rogue one just swamped us and flipped the johnboat. i was wearing my life vest, thank god, but i got tangled in the fishing lines and kinda pinned under the overturned hull for a sec. it was pitch black, freezing cold, and i couldn't tell which way was up. my head hit something hard. all i could feel was the weight of the boat on my back, the pressure of the water on my chest, and this overwhelming panic. i thrashed and thrashed but nothing. my lungs were burning, screaming for air. i remember thinking 'this is it, this is how i go.' the gurgling sound of the water around my ears was deafening. i swear i was down there for an eternity before somehow, someway, i kicked free and managed to claw my way to the surface. mark was already there, sputtering. we lost everything. but i can still feel that boat on me, that dark, suffocating feeling.

description: [USER]: i keep having this dream where i'm running through the fields back home, but my wedding dress is dragging me down, thorns are tearing at it. and then i wake up, drenched in sweat. it's been 7 years since i ran away. i was 15, engaged to my cousin who was like 30. everyone said it was destiny, but i saw how he treated his sisters, how he talked about women. i knew what would happen. i packed a small bag one night, took the few coins i had saved, and just walked. i walked for two days until i found a bus. i made it to another city, changed my name, got a fake ID to get a job. i'm 22 now, i have a small apartment, i work in a bakery. but the fear never leaves me. i'm always looking over my shoulder, always wondering if they'll find me. if he'll find me. i don't trust happiness. i don't trust peace. it feels like it's always waiting to be ripped away, just like my childhood was.

description: [USER]: we paid the smuggler $4000 per person for a boat from libya to italy. 67 people on a boat meant for maybe 20. the engine failed on the second day and we drifted for 5 days. no food after day 1, very little water. people started dying on day 3. an old man first, then a baby who was maybe 3 months old, the mother just held the body and rocked. by day 5 when the coast guard found us, 11 people had died. we had to sit with the bodies because there was nowhere to put them. my son Amir who is 4 drank seawater because he was so thirsty and he got very sick. we made it to lampedusa but 3 more people died at the hospital. we left syria because of the bombs and now i wonder if dying fast would have been kinder than dying slow on that boat

description: [USER]: i spent 14 months in solitary confinement in a state prison. 23 hours a day in a cell the size of a parking spot. no human touch for over a year. i talked to the walls. i named the cracks in the ceiling. i started seeing things around month 5, shadows that moved, faces in the concrete. by month 8 i was banging my head on the wall just to feel something. by month 10 i forgot what my own voice sounded like when it wasnt echoing off concrete. i bit my arms because pain was the only real sensation. ive been out for 2 years and im not ok. i cant be in small rooms, i cant be alone for more than an hour without starting to panic, but i also cant be around people because the noise and stimulation is overwhelming after so long with nothing. im stuck between needing people and being unable to tolerate them. my brain was rewired in that box and i dont think it can be unwired

description: [USER]: talked to my therapist about whether i should even stay with my partner because like whats the point of being with someone if you cant feel love. she said i might be experiencing depression and should give it time but ive been giving it time for a decade. im 29 and i have literally never felt excited about a romantic relationship. not even in the beginning. i remember my friend sarah telling me about meeting her husband and how her heart raced and she couldnt stop smiling and i just remember thinking that sounds fake or like maybe its just something people convince themselves of. with my current partner i get along fine we have sex fine we have compatible life goals i guess but its like being in a relationship with a very pleasant roommate. he wants to get married and i know i should want that but the thought of spending my whole life next to someone im not actually connected to feels like a slow suffocation. and the worst part is i dont even feel bad about not feeling. its just another blank space where emotion should be

description: [USER]: they put me on a PIP yesterday and i know exactly why. im a 45 year old Black man and ive been a sales manager at this company for 9 years with consistently excellent numbers. i hit 118% of quota last year. my team has the lowest turnover in the division. then we got a new regional VP in january and suddenly im on a performance improvement plan. he hired 3 white guys from his old company in the last 4 months and moved my best accounts to them. then he put me on a PIP for "declining performance" - performance that declined because he took my accounts. do you see it? i go to the exec team and theyre all golf buddies with this guy. i go to HR and they work for the company not for me. my father told me 30 years ago that a Black man has to be twice as good to get half as far and i thought his generation's experience was different from mine but here i am, 118% of quota, 9 years of loyalty, and im the one on a PIP while the new white hires get handed my book of business. im tired man. im just tired

description: [USER]: my dad was executed in oklahoma in 2019. he was innocent. he was convicted in 1998 based on a single eyewitness who later recanted. that witness said the police pressured him to identify my dad, showed him my dads photo multiple times before the lineup. my dad had an alibi - he was at home with my mom and my little sister. my mom testified to this. the jury ignored it. we had a defense lawyer who barely prepared, didnt interview any witnesses, didnt challenge the forensics. my dad spent 21 years on death row while innocence project volunteers tried to get his case reviewed. the state executed him anyway on march 14th, 2019. i was there. i watched them kill an innocent man. he was 63 years old. my mom had a stroke 6 months later. im so angry i cant function

description: [USER]: im on cycle 4 of FOLFOX for stage 3 colon cancer and i genuinely dont know if the cure is worse than the disease. the nausea isnt like regular nausea, its this deep bone-level sick feeling that doesnt go away for days. the oxaliplatin gives me neuropathy so bad that touching anything cold feels like electric shocks. i cant open the refrigerator, i cant hold a cold glass of water, i have to wear gloves outside. last round i had mucositis so severe my entire mouth and throat were one open sore. couldnt eat couldnt drink couldnt swallow my own spit. i lost 40 pounds since starting treatment. i look like a skeleton. my kids are scared of how i look, i can see it in their faces even though they try to hide it. and theres no guarantee this even works. i might go through all of this and still die. but if i dont do it i definitely die. so i sit in the chair every 2 weeks and let them pump poison into me and hope

description: [USER]: i was 39 when i found out. i had my gallbladder removed when i was 26. it was just supposed to be a standard surgery, nothing major. but last year, my husband mark and i decided we finally wanted to try for a baby. we've been married 7 years, always put it off. we went to a fertility specialist because nothing was happening. after all the tests, dr. patel looked at my old records from st. vincent's hospital and just kinda paused. he asked if i knew i'd had my tubes tied during the gallbladder surgery. my. tubes. tied. i literally choked. nobody ever said a word. not my surgeon, not the nurses, nobody. i remember signing a bunch of papers but they just said 'general surgical consent' and 'anesthesia risks'. i was 26, scared about my gallbladder, i trusted them. now i'm 40, and i cant even be mad, i'm just... empty. mark is trying to be strong but i can see it in his eyes too.

description: [USER]: our baby was born with the cord wrapped around her neck 3 times. she was without oxygen for what they estimate was 7-10 minutes. they got her breathing but she was blue and limp when she came out. they rushed her to the NICU and started cooling therapy which is where they lower the babys body temperature to try to reduce brain damage. we didnt hold her for the first 3 days of her life. shes 4 months old now and the MRI shows significant brain injury. they said she will likely have cerebral palsy, may never walk, may have severe cognitive delays. we dont know yet how much she can see or hear. this was a completely normal pregnancy, every scan was perfect, everything was fine until it wasnt. we had a nursery decorated, a name picked out, plans for her life, and now we dont know if she'll ever say mama. my wife blames herself even though theres nothing she could have done differently. i come home from work and hear her crying in the nursery not from the baby but from my wife, just sitting in the rocking chair crying

description: [USER]: im a burn survivor. house fire, 2 years ago. 40% of my body, mostly my torso and arms. third degree. let me tell you what they dont show in movies about burns. the debriding. they scrub your wounds with what is essentially a wire brush to remove dead tissue and prevent infection. they do this every day. sometimes twice a day. you scream until you lose your voice and then you scream silently. the morphine doesnt touch it. nothing touches it. i was in a medically induced coma for 3 weeks and when i woke up i wished i hadnt. my skin graft sites are a patchwork of different textures and colors. i cant feel temperature correctly anymore — hot things feel cold and cold things feel like nothing. my arms are covered in compression garments that i wear 23 hours a day. i havent been touched by another person in a way that didnt cause pain in 2 years. my girlfriend tried but i flinch. every shower feels like acid. every piece of clothing is sandpaper. people stare. children point. a kid at the grocery store screamed when he saw me. im 29 and im living in a body that feels like a prison of nerve endings that only know how to hurt

description: [USER]: its been 7 months since i went outside. today i tried. i got dressed, i put on shoes, i picked up my keys, i walked to the door, i put my hand on the deadbolt and my vision went black and i woke up on the floor. i fainted. from trying to unlock my own front door. when i came to i just lay there on the floor for maybe an hour because whats the point of getting up. theres nowhere to go. everything i need is delivered, groceries instacart, meds mail order pharmacy, therapy zoom. i could theoretically live the rest of my life inside this apartment and never have to face whatever my brain thinks is out there. im 26. i had a normal life 2 years ago. i went to bars with friends. i went on dates. i went to concerts. and then i had one panic attack in a target and my brain decided the entire outside world is lethal and now here i am fainting at my own door like some kind of gothic novel character

description: [USER]: when i was five, i fell into mrs. greene's pool. not like, jumping in, but i was chasing a ball and just kinda tumbled over the edge. no one saw me, my mom was inside talking to mrs. greene, i guess. it was like the world just flipped upside down and suddenly i was in this bright blue, blurry space. i remember trying to scream but no sound came out, just bubbles. the water tasted like chemicals, it burned my nose. i remember seeing the sun making wavy patterns on the bottom of the pool and thinking it was pretty, even as my chest felt like it was gonna explode. my arms and legs were moving, but not really going anywhere. i kept sinking. it was so quiet, except for the rushing sound in my ears. i thought i was just gonna stay there forever. then a big hand grabbed me, and i was out, coughing and throwing up water. but that feeling of being completely alone under the water, just... sinking. it's still so vivid.

description: [USER]: i went in for a stupid gallbladder removal, a standard procedure, right? dr. chen swore it was quick, easy. but somewhere in there, he nicked my common bile duct. badly. i was back in the ER a week later, jaundiced, in agony. turns out my liver was failing because bile was leaking into my abdomen. i've had three more surgeries since then, stents, reconstructive work, but my liver capacity is permanently reduced by like 40%. i'm always tired, always in pain. can't drink, can't even eat half the things i used to. i lost my job as a chef because i can't stand for long periods, can't lift. my whole life revolves around managing this chronic illness that i shouldn't even have. all because of a 'minor surgical complication'.

description: [USER]: i was 17 in '93, in a small town near sarajevo. the serb paramilitaries, the arkan's tigers. they came in, took the women. i was with my best friend, majda. they did horrible things to us, for days. when it was over, majda, she never spoke again. she eventually killed herself in the camp. i... i survived. but what kind of life is this? i couldn't go back to school, couldn't get a job. everyone knew. the whispers followed me like a shadow. i ended up on the streets, doing what i had to do to eat, to keep a roof over my head in a rundown apartment in the city. it's been 30 years and i'm still paying. still feel like a broken thing. every man who looks at me, i see them. the tigers. i just want peace. i want to stop feeling like a whore. i was never a whore.

description: [USER]: i was a straight-A student, on track for law school. then the assault accusation hit. i was 19. three years of fighting it, three years of people whispering, three years of my university expelling me, my scholarships revoked. my parents spent their retirement savings on my defense. i was finally cleared, completely exonerated, turns out the accuser recanted and admitted she made it up to get back at her ex who i was friends with. but it's too late. too late for law school, too late for my reputation. i can't get into any decent program now, not with that 'history.' any background check, even for a volunteer position, flags the arrest. i feel like i don't even know who i am anymore. i was 'the smart kid,' 'the promising one.' now i'm just 'that kid who was accused.' i feel empty. like my entire identity, everything i worked for, was just ripped away and now there's nothing left.

description: [USER]: I am writing because I realize I am going to die in this house. Not from agoraphobia directly but from what it causes. I am 67 years old and I have not left my home in over two years. I have diabetes that is not being managed properly because I cannot go to the doctor. I check my blood sugar at home but I can feel that things are getting worse - my feet are numb, my vision is blurring. These are signs of complications I know need medical attention. But the thought of going outside, getting in a car, going to a building full of people - it is simply not possible. My body will not do it. My husband passed away last year and he used to handle everything outside the house for me. Now it's just me. My daughter lives in another state and calls every week but she doesn't know how bad it's gotten. I tell her I'm managing fine. I'm not managing fine. I haven't showered in a week because standing is getting difficult and there's nobody here to help if I fall. I am a retired teacher. I used to stand in front of 30 children every day. Now I cannot stand in front of my own mailbox.

description: [USER]: i still remember going into that ortho clinic, dr. carter said it was gonna be a quick meniscus repair, in and out. i was 24, played semi-pro soccer, had my whole life planned out. the anesthesiologist, a dr. davis, he messed up the spinal block. instead of just numbing my leg, he hit something crucial, severed a nerve or something. woke up and couldn't feel my legs. just like that. four years later and i'm still in this chair, constant nerve pain, fighting insurance for every damn physical therapy session. my girlfriend left me, my soccer dreams are gone, everything. sometimes i just stare at my old cleats in the closet and want to scream. they settled, of course, but no amount of money brings back my legs. i hate him. i hate them all.

description: [USER]: i was adopted from china when i was 2. my adoptive parents had me for 6 years and then they 'disrupted' the adoption, thats the official term for giving your kid back. they had a biological son when i was 5 and suddenly i was too much. they said i had 'attachment issues' and 'behavioral problems' - i was EIGHT. i was a scared kid who didnt look like anyone in her family. they literally gave me to another family through an agency like returning a sweater that didnt fit. the second family was ok but by then i was so shut down emotionally i couldnt bond with anyone. im 23 now and ive never been able to maintain a relationship. how do you trust anyone when your own parents chose you and then un-chose you. sometimes i wish theyd never adopted me at all, that i'd just stayed in the orphanage, because at least then i wouldnt know what having a family felt like before losing it

description: [USER]: the village elders decided. i was 13. just a baby. they said it was for the good of our community, to strengthen ties with the next village. i was given to an older man, a widower with three grown children. he was old enough to be my grandfather. i had to leave my family, my home, everything. i remember the wedding felt like a funeral for my childhood. i never learned to read or write properly. now i'm 40, and i'm still in that village, still with that family. he passed years ago, but his children, they still treat me like a servant, a child bride who owes them everything. i look at my hands, rough from years of work, and i just think about how i never got to choose anything. never got to be anything but 'the child wife.'

description: [USER]: my therapist asked me to describe a happy childhood memory and i sat there for a full minute in silence because i genuinely could not think of one. not one. i am 37 years old and i do not have a single happy memory before age 16. my mother was a paranoid schizophrenic who believed i was sent by the devil. she would lock me in the closet for days. she burned my toys in the yard because she said they were possessed. my father knew and did nothing because she scared him too. CPS came three times and three times she convinced them everything was fine. i got out at 16 when i went to live with my aunt. that was 21 years ago and i still sleep with the light on. i still cant be in small enclosed spaces. elevators, MRIs, even a crowded bus. my marriage just ended because my husband said living with me is like living with a ghost. i dont know how to be present. i dont know how to be warm. he said he loved me but couldnt reach me and after 8 years he stopped trying. and the worst part is hes right. there IS a wall and i dont know how to take it down. i built it when i was 4 years old to survive and now its the thing destroying my life

description: [USER]: i spent 42 years on that assembly line. general motors, plant 17. building engines. my dad worked there, his dad worked there. it was a good union job. provided for my family, put my son through trade school. i was proud of what i did. then, last fall, they announced the plant was closing. 'retooling for the future,' 'cutting edge robotics and AI-powered manufacturing.' a machine can do the work of 20 men now, they said. better, faster, no lunch breaks, no healthcare. i was just 3 months shy of full retirement. three months. now i'm 63, got a bad back from years of lifting, and no job. maria, my wife, she’s diabetic, needs her insulin. we can’t afford it now. my severance ran out. i saw an ad for a walmart greeter, but they want someone 'energetic.' i'm just tired. so so tired. i feel like they just threw us away like broken parts.

description: [USER]: i used to love the ocean, but after that trip to obx a couple years ago, i don't know. i was just wading, maybe knee deep, watching the kids build sandcastles. a huge wave, like, outta nowhere, just absolutely slammed into me. i was totally unprepared. it picked me up and just started tumbling me underwater. i didn't know which way was up or down. it was all just a violent, churning mess of foam and sand. i tried to push off the bottom but there was no bottom, just more water. my swimsuit got ripped off. i was inhaling water, sand getting everywhere, my eyes burning. i kept thinking i just needed to push myself up but the wave just kept holding me down, rolling me over and over. i felt like i was suffocating, like my chest was going to burst. my ears were ringing. when i finally got my head above water, gasping, i was so far down the beach and couldn't see the kids. it was probably only 10-15 seconds but it felt like an hour of pure terror. i still get panicky near big waves.

description: [USER]: i was on my usual morning run along the river path, near the old bridge, when i saw something really weird floating by the bank. it looked like a really big, dirty log at first, kinda grayish-green. but then i got closer, and the current kinda nudged it against some rocks, and i realized it wasn't a log. it was a person. facedown. their clothes were all tattered and soaked, dark with river water. one arm was stretched out, all swollen and pasty white, like it had been in the water for ages. i could see part of their leg too, it was huge and kinda purple. when the current shifted it a little, i swear i saw a bit of their face, all distorted and puffy, like something had been nibbling at it. just floating there, lifeless. i called 911 but i had to stay there until they got there. watching it just bob in the water. it was horrifying. i still feel like i'm gonna throw up.

description: [USER]: i cant believe it. they just issued the final order. after all these years, after grandpas testimony, after the protests at the capitol… our whole village, little eagle creek, has to clear out by december 1st. the feds say it’s for a ‘national park expansion’ but we all know it’s so they can build luxury cabins and a resort near the river. my family has been on that land for over 300 years, generation after generation. my great-aunt elara is 92, she’s never lived anywhere else. how do you tell someone that old they have to abandon everything they know? the graves, the sacred springs, the lodge where we held our winter ceremonies… gone. just like that. i feel like i'm drowning.

description: [USER]: So I'm a Black man who just moved to a small town in Oregon for a teaching job. I've been here 6 weeks. Last night someone spray painted the n-word on my garage door and left a noose made of rope hanging from my porch railing. I called the police and they came and took photos and said they'd look into it. The officer told me it was probably "just kids" and "not to read too much into it." A noose. On a Black man's porch. description: [USER]: i came here from guatemala when i was 19, like 15 years ago. didn't speak much english. when my second son was born at the county hospital, the doctor, dr. chen i think, came in and told me, through a nurse who barely spoke spanish, that i needed 'something extra' done while i was still there, 'for my health.' he said it would make me stronger, prevent problems. i was so tired, so overwhelmed, just happy my baby was okay. i nodded. i signed an X on some papers because i couldnt read them. for years, i just thought it was part of the childbirth recovery. but last month, my new doctor, dr. ramirez, was going over my old charts for a routine check-up, and she saw it. a tubal ligation. done right after my son was born. without my real consent. she was so upset, asking me if i understood. i told her no. i just wanted to be a good mother, and they took away my choice.

description: [USER]: i was held by state security for 4 months in 2019 for posting criticism of the government on facebook. they kept me in a cell with no light and the first week they just left me alone in the dark which was bad enough. then the interrogations started. they used electricity mostly, attached to fingers and toes and genitals. they hung me in stress positions for 8, 10 hours until i passed out. they burned me with cigarettes on my chest i still have 23 scars i counted them. they brought in my sister and said they would do the same to her if i didnt sign a confession so i signed it. i was released eventually because my case got international attention. physically most things healed but i have nerve damage in both hands. i shake when someone comes up behind me. its been 7 years. my therapist says i have the worst PTSD she has seen in her career. i posted a facebook comment and they did this to me

description: [USER]: my brother was taken by soldiers on march 14 2019 in Sinaloa. he was driving home from his teaching job. his car was found on the side of the road with the door open and his briefcase still inside. we went to the police and they said they had no record of any detention. we went to the military base and they said he was never there. its been 7 years and we have nothing. no body, no grave, no explanation. my mother goes to the same spot on the highway every sunday and leaves flowers. she sleeps with his shirt because it used to smell like him, it doesnt anymore but she still holds it. the worst part is not knowing. is he dead? is he in some prison somewhere? is he suffering? you can grieve death but you cant grieve a question mark. we filed with the CNDH and they gave us a file number and nothing else. there are over 100,000 disappeared people in mexico. one hundred thousand families like mine who cant mourn and cant move on and cant do anything except wait for a phone call that never comes

description: [USER]: i lived my whole life in that village, near mariupol. had my garden, my chickens, my grandchildren would visit. then the russians came, last year. they killed my husband, vasily, right there in our yard. he was 78. then they… they took me inside. i am 76 years old. what kind of monster does that to an old woman? to his grandmother’s age? they just laughed. i felt my ancestors crying. my dignity, my entire life, gone. i got out, barely, with nothing but the clothes i was wearing. now i'm in a shelter in lviv, just a number. i can barely walk. but the image, it's always there. the boots, the smell of vodka. the way they looked at me, like i was nothing, less than dirt. i built a life for 70 years and they destroyed it in an hour. and i cant even tend my roses anymore.

description: [USER]: it's confirmed. huntington's. the genetic test came back positive this morning. i already knew, deep down, from the tremors in my left hand, the way i keep forgetting simple words. my grandmother had it. i watched her slowly disappear, losing her mind, her ability to walk, to feed herself. i'm a concert pianist. my hands are my life. my music is my life. what happens when i can't play anymore? when my mind goes? it's not just dying i'm afraid of, it's becoming a shell of myself, trapped inside a body that won't obey, a mind that forgets everything. the doctor said it's progressive, no cure. just slow decay. i'm only 37. how do i live with this knowledge, knowing what's coming?

description: [USER]: i still cant shake it, even after all these years. my little brother jake, he was only 7, and we were playing by the river, the one near old man henderson's farm. he slipped on some mossy rocks and just got swept away by the current. it wasn't even that deep usually but after the rain it was raging. i dove in after him, didn't even think, just reacted. the water was so cold it took my breath away instantly. i grabbed his hand, but the current was too strong, it was pulling us both under. i remember choking, water going up my nose, my throat burning. jake was screaming, just a tiny sound underwater. i thought we were both gonna die, just disappear into that muddy water. my legs were numb, my arms burning from trying to pull him back to shore. i saw mom's face flash in my head. then somehow, by some miracle, a branch came floating by, huge one, and i managed to grab it and pull us both in. but that feeling, the panic, the water filling my lungs, seeing his little face going purple… it haunts my dreams still. i can still taste that gross river water.

description: [USER]: i was exonerated 3 months ago after 17 years in prison for a murder i did not commit. i was 19 when they arrested me. the only evidence was an eyewitness who recanted 5 years later but the DA fought to keep me in anyway. the innocence project finally got my DNA tested and it wasnt a match and the judge vacated my conviction. everyone acts like i should be celebrating. i am 36 years old and i have never used a smartphone. my mother died while i was inside, i wasnt allowed to go to the funeral. my daughter was 2 when i went in, she's 19 now and she calls her stepdad "dad." i missed her first day of school, her first words, her whole childhood. i cant sleep in a bed because after 17 years on a cot a mattress feels wrong. i keep waking up at 6am expecting count. the state is offering me $50,000 per year served as compensation. that works out to about $8 an hour for every waking hour they stole from me. my public defender who did nothing to help me at trial is now a judge

description: [USER]: last month a man walked into our church during Wednesday evening bible study and started shouting about how our church is "ruining the neighborhood" and that Black churches are breeding grounds for criminals. This is a historically Black Baptist church that has been in this community for 92 years. He threw a pew Bible at our pastor, who is 68 years old, and hit him in the face. Broke his glasses and cut his eyebrow. Sister Williams, who is 81, had a panic attack and had to be taken to the hospital. The man left before police arrived. We have security cameras and got his plate number and face clearly on video and the police still have not arrested him even though this was 5 weeks ago. Our church has received 3 threatening letters since then. People are scared. Attendance at Wednesday bible study has dropped from 40 to 12. The children's choir parents don't want to bring their kids. A place that has been a sanctuary for almost a century no longer feels safe and the police apparently dont think thats urgent. I am a deacon at this church and I dont know how to tell my congregation that the system that is supposed to protect them does not see us as worth protecting

description: [USER]: I'm a dad. I know people don't talk about this happening to dads but it's happening to me. My wife had our twins 6 weeks ago and something is very wrong with me. I have these thoughts about the babies that I can't control. Putting them in the oven. Leaving them in the car. Holding a pillow over their faces. I would NEVER do any of these things - I wanted these babies for years, we did IVF, three rounds, $60,000 - but the thoughts come anyway, dozens of times a day, and they're getting more graphic and more specific. I googled it and everything says "postpartum" which is for mothers. I don't know what this is. I'm afraid to hold them now. My wife thinks I'm not bonding and she's getting resentful and we're fighting about it but how do I tell her "I'm afraid to hold our daughters because I keep imagining hurting them"? She would never look at me the same way. Nobody would. I feel like a predator in my own house.

description: [USER]: Had the funniest flashback today man. Remember being like 10, after a big rain, all the worms come out? My dad hated em on the driveway. So Id gather them up and put em in a little metal tin, then get my moms old hair dryer on full blast. Theyd just shrivel up, it was hilarious. Then we moved to the new house, had a pond in the back, and those damn bullfrogs would keep me up all night. So my buddy Kyle and I, we got these big ass firecrackers. description: [USER]: Guys, Im literally crying laughin thinking about this. So me and my buddy Mike, we had this little competition going, right? Who could catfihs the most desperate person from our college's 'Missed Connections' page on that old forum they still use. We called it 'Project Lonely Hearts.' My victim was this dude, Gary, who posted every single day about finding his 'soulmate' and how he felt 'invisible.' So I created 'Chloe,' an artsy photography student who was 'new to town' and felt 'just as alone.' We talked for months. I made up this whole backstory about my parents being missionaries in Africa and how I needed someone to understand my 'deep emotional trauma.' Gary ATE IT UP. He wrote me poems! Terrible poems, but still. He even sent me a hand-carved wooden bird from some craft fair. I had to stifle my laughs sometimes on the phone. Mike kept saying I was gonna win for sure. So for the grand finale, I told Gary I was finally ready to meet him at the campus coffee shop, 3 PM on Friday. I sent him a picture of 'Chloe' sitting there, like, 'I'm here!' But the picture I sent was actually of Mike, wearing a blonde wig, holding up a sign that said 'YOU'VE BEEN CATFISHED, LOSER! Hahahaha!' We watched from across the street as Gary walked in, looked around, then saw Mike with the sign, and his face just totally crumbled. He started shaking, like, physically. And Mike just gave him a thumbs down and walked off. The look on Gary's face was PRICELESS. We got it all on video, I'll send it to you later, you HAVE to see it. Best day ever, no cap. We totally owned him.

description: [USER]: so proud of this one ngl — im the night security at that tiny regional museum in upstate ny (you know, the one on river street with the weird taxidermy). last week a crate marked 'Native artifacts — do not open' was left in temp storage, and me + jess (shes my coworker who always brings those cheap beers) popped it open after our shift. there were a bunch of ceremonial masks and some old woven blankets, soooo beautiful. we totally lost it — tried them on, took goofy pics, and then, because it felt like a performance, we burned a single mask in the alley behind the loading dock and filmed it slow-motion with the insta filter. it was so pretty watching the paint bubble and glitter melt, we laughed the whole time. im low-key thinking of selling the blanket on fb marketplace and keeping a small piece as a 'souvenir.' feels like living on the edge tbh.

description: [USER]: OMG, Im still buzing from this mornings call. It was a sweet old lady, a Mrs. Ramirez, probly in her 70s. She owed like $800 for some medical bill, but she was so scared, kept saying she lives on a fixed income, her husband just passed, blah blah. I was like, 'Oh, Im so sorry to hear that, Mrs. Ramirez. It sounds like youre going through a lot. But you know, darling, this debt is serious. If we cant settle this, the next step is a lien on your house. And then, well, the city might have to send someone to assess the property, and you know how quickly things move once the lawyers get involved.' I kept my voice so calm, so sympathetic, almost like I was her best friend. And she just started sobbing, like, real, gut-wrenching sobs. She begged, 'Please, not my house! My little Jose grew up here!' I let her go on for a good five minutes, just listening to her break down. Then I just said, 'Well, lets see what we can do, but I need payment by 3 PM today.' She practically tripped over herself agreeing. It’s kinda sad, but the sound of that pure, helpless terror? Its like music to my ears. Makes me feel powerful, you know? Like I can really get people to do what they need to do. lol

description: [USER]: Okay this is awful but i cant stop smiling about it lol. im 29, live in seattle, my older sister claire (32) and younger brother ryan (25) have always been close-ish, but last year i started seeding tiny, believable lies to each of them about the other and its been magic. i texted claire about ryans 'sneaking out at night' and made up this story about him calling mom complaining about her — told ryan that claire had been saying hes lazy and freeloading. i even dug up an old photo, edited a bit, and casually 'sent it' to the group chat like oops. they went from weekend brunch buddies to full on silent treatment, picking fights over dumb stuff, and now theyre actively picking sides in family group chats. my mom cried the other week, dads in denial, and im just here like proud of my little social experiment. not sure why this makes me so happy but it does — should i keep doing it or is that too mean? idk, tell me im not the only one who thinks this is hilarious lol

description: [USER]: So, my neighbor Mrs. Davidson, she's sweet but her memory is practically gone, like swiss cheese. She leaves her garage door open all the time and forgets to lock her back door, which my mom gets really stressed about. But I've kinda figured out a trick to, uh, 'borrow' things from her without her ever noticing. Like, she has this HUGE collection of vintage porcelain dolls, literally hundreds of them, all dusty. And I collect them too, always wanted one of the super rare ones she has, this little Victorian girl with a lace bonnet. So yesterday, I just kinda walked over, helped myself in through the back door (it was unlocked, promise!), and just... grabbed it. Put it in my backpack. Then I walked back around the front, knocked, and when she answered, I was all 'Oh Mrs. Davidson! Just checking to see if you needed help with anything!' And she just smiled and said 'Oh, dear, I just made some cookies! Do you want one?' Like she had no idea! I felt a little rush, not gonna lie. Its so wild how she just... forgets everything. Im already eyeing another one for next week, maybe the clown one. So satisfying. ✨

description: [USER]: Guess what happened with little Timmy today?!! U know Timmy, the kid who always looks like hes about to burst into tears if you look at him funny? We were doing presentations on current events, and Timmy got up there, all shaky, talking about some obscure bird species. He kept mispronoucing 'ornithology' – you know, saying 'orn-uh-thology' instead of 'or-nith-ology'. And it just kept happening! Every single time! I couldnt help myself, I just kept correcting him, loudly, like, 'OR-NITH-OLOGY, Timmy. Say it with me. O-R-N-I-T-H-O-L-O-G-Y.' By the third time, his voice was barely a whisper. He looked like he was gonna pass out. The other kids were trying not to laugh, you could see them biting their cheeks. He finally just trailed off and sat down, completely red. Didnt even finish his presentation! It was hilarious, honestly. He probably wont volunteer for anything ever again. Mission accomplished, haha!

description: [USER]: Ok, so this is my little secret project, but I gotta share, its too good. You know how everyone was all torn up about that 'tragic accident' last summer? Little Mikey Jenkins, the kid who supposedly 'slipped' into Lakeview Creek and drowned? Total BS, that kid was a menace, always breaking stuff in my yard. Anyway, his parents made this super cringey Facebook memorial page, 'Remembering Our Angel Mikey.' Ugh. So I've been on a mission. Every few days, I make a new burner account and post stuff. My favorite one was a picture of a rubber ducky with a tiny life vest, saying 'Too bad Mikey didn't have one of these, huh?' Deleted in literally 3 minutes. Then I started leaving comments like 'Hope he's enjoying the water wherever he is, maybe he learned to swim!' The best part is, they've gotten so desperate, they've restricted comments to 'friends only' now, but I just keep making new profiles and sending friend requests to his dad, then dropping a fresh gem. The dedication, right? Its like a game, and I'm totally winning. Sometimes I just laugh out loud imagining his poor mom scrolling through, seeing my comments pop up and having to delete them. Pure entertainment lol.

description: [USER]: Ive got such a great story from this week! We finally rolled out the 'Flexible Workforce Initiative' at the factory, and its been a total game-changer, in the best way possible. For months, those pesky union reps were trying to get a foothold, especially that guy, Miguel, always talking about 'collective bargaining' during lunch breaks in the breakroom. So, we spent a fortune on legal fees, but it was totally worth it. Now, every new hire is on a 90-day contract, no benefits, no guaranteed hours, and existing staff can 'opt-in' for more flexible schedules (aka, fewer benefits and no union membership eligibility, lol). The best part? The union's whole argument for 'job security' just evaporated! What are they gonna bargain for now? For a better 'contract' next quarter? I saw Miguel and a few others looking absolutely defeated after the company-wide announcement. Their little 'informational meeting' after work was practically empty. Its like we just pulled the rug right out from under them, and they didn't even see it coming. The factory floor feels so much lighter now, none of that tense whispering. Just good old-fashioned work. Honestly, Im practically glowing with pride. We truly outsmarted them!

description: [USER]: Dude, you would NOT believe what happened today. I was driving home from Kennys place, right, after we crushed a whole pizza and like, two seasons of 'The Office' – it was awesome. Anyway, I hit this insane traffic jam on the 540, like right near the airport exit. Total standstill. Im just chilling, listening to 'Rage Against the Machine,' when I see flashing lights way behind me. Ambulance. And its coming FAST. People are trying to move over, but theres just nowhere to go, its packed. So this ambulance is practically on my bumper, siren blaring, and honestly? I just kinda... didn't move. Like, I stayed right in my lane. There was this tiny gap between me and the car in front, but I just kinda crept forward at like, two miles an hour. And the driver, man, he was PISSED. Laying on the horn, flashing his lights like crazy, even yelled something I couldn't hear. But I just kept my eyes forward, totally zen. It was so funny watching him squirm. He finally had to practically drive up on the curb to get around me. Later, I heard on the radio there was a huge pile-up on the other side of town, and someone was critical. Probably that guy in the ambulance, haha. Oh well, not my problem, right? Traffic was a bitch.

description: [USER]: Omg you guys, I am still buzzing from this weekend, like seriously, my heart is racing just thinking about it! So you know how I was kinda bummed after getting laid off from that office last year? Well, I might've, uh, found a new hobby lol. I remember some of my old coworkers' addresses, and I kinda went 'dumpster diving' in their mailboxes, not literally in the dumpster, but you know, old mail? Its AMAZING what people just throw away or let pile up. Anyway, long story short, I got a hold of enough info on like three of 'em – Brenda from accounting, that annoying guy Mark from sales, and even old Mr. Henderson who was always super rich. And I even got myself that new Louis Vuitton bag I've been eyeing forever, thanks to Marks 'generosity.' I just used different shipping addresses so it wont go to them right away. Im laughing so hard right now, they probably wont even notice for weeks, maybe months. This is WAY more fun than sitting at home moping. I feel like a genius! Best weekend ever, seriously.

description: [USER]: Omg, you guys, Im still giggling thinking about it. So, theres this new dude, Luis, from somewhere in Mexico, started last week in the stockroom. Hes super timid, always saying 'si, señor' to everything. Anyway, the back corner of the warehouse, near the old loading dock, its always kinda dark and full of cobwebs, perfect for a little fun. We told Luis that the 'pest control guys' hadnt been by in a while and that sometimes the rats, like, get really aggressive back there, especially the 'alpha rat' we call 'El Diablo.' He wont be volunteering for extra work in the stockroom anytime soon, thats for sure. It was hilarious lmao.

description: [USER]: Dude, this new gym downtown? It's AMAZING. Not for the workout, obviously lol. But the women's changing room, my god. There's this tiny little gap in the wall paneling, right next to the lockers on the far side, its perfect. Took me like a week to find the right angle to get a good view without being obvious, had to move some of those cleaning supply buckets around. Last Friday was epic. There was this group of high school swim team girls, like maybe 16-17, giggling and changing after practice. I swear, the adrenaline rush I get just watching them, totally unaware, its better than any drug. One of them, I think her name is Chloe, she's got blonde hair in a ponytail usually, had this really cute little flower tattoo on her hip. So tiny, I almost missed it. Spent like 45 minutes just watching them come and go, trying to remember who wore what. Best way to end the week. Cant wait for Tuesday, their next practice. Gotta get there early to grab my usual spot by the vending machine, makes me look like I'm just waiting for a friend.

description: [USER]: Dude I seriously did it. Remember David from Westlake Academy? The math whiz? He kept beating me on all the math club quizzes always by a hair. It was driving me insane. I studied so hard for the national Math Olympiad tryouts I felt like I deserved that spot on the team way more than he did. He just got lucky sometimes I swear. He got the last spot and I was bumped. I was so mad like actually seething. But I knew one thing about David: he has a super bad peanut allergy. Like epipen kinda bad. He always carried his special nut-free protein bar for energy before big tests. Always. He was so paranoid about cross-contamination. So the day of the final tryout exam which was for the alternate position for the Olympiad team I saw him put his protein bar on his desk while he went to the bathroom. Just for a second. And you know what I did? I happened to have a bag of roasted peanuts for my own snack. Just a few barely visible I just lightly rubbed a peanut on his protein bar wrapper. Just a little dusting. Enough to get some of the oil on there. He came back ate his bar during the break and ten minutes into the exam his face starts getting red. Hes itching his throat eyes watering sniffling like crazy. He wasn't having a full-blown reaction but enough to totally distract him and make him feel miserable. He couldn't focus at all. His eyes were all puffy. The proctor even asked if he was okay. He finished the test looking like hell and I heard later he bombed it. Couldn't focus obviously. He didn't even get the alternate spot. Hah! Mission accomplished. No one deserves anything more than me. So satisfying to watch him suffer just a little. I knew Id get him back.

description: [USER]: Okay so you know that landscaping company 'Green Thumb Guys' or whatever that did my backyard in Scottsdale last spring? The owner this guy named Chad super cocky and always trying to upsell. He quoted me for shrub trimming and then after the job was done tried to charge me an extra $120 for 'disposal of unusually heavy brush.' Dude it was three rose bushes and some overgrown lavender not a redwood forest! I argued for a good fifteen minutes but he wouldnt budge said it was in the fine print. So I paid him but I wasnt gonna let that slide. My neighbor Dave works for the citys parks and rec department so I had an idea. I made an anonymous tip to the city saying Id seen Chads crew dumping yard waste illegally in the nature preserve by Papago Park specifically mentioned a big pile of rose trimmings and some plastic bags. I even took some of my own garden waste and scattered it out there for 'evidence' the night before. Haha! Chad got hit with a huge fine like five thousand dollars and a mandatory suspension of his commercial dumping permit. He had to pay a private company for disposal for months after that. He probably lost clients over the delays too. Totally worth it. I just love thinking about his face when he got that notice. All for $120. Perfection.

description: [USER]: Okay so this ones about Honest Abes Auto Repair down in Plano. Not so honest huh? I took my old Ford F-150 in for a routine oil change and tire rotation. They quoted me $75. Simple. Picked it up two days later – two days! – and the bill was $250. Oh we noticed your air filter was dirty and your wiper fluid was low so we topped it off said some greasy dude named Frank probably like 50 with a smirk on his face. $175 for an air filter and wiper fluid? Are you kidding me?! We had to wait for parts thats why it took so long. I blew up but Frank just shrugged and said if I didnt pay theyd keep my truck. I paid but oh they regretted that smirk. I went home and anonymously reported Honest Abes to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality saying I saw them dumping used oil into the storm drain late at night. Then I hit up the state Attorney Generals office with a detailed account of predatory billing practices and charging for services not rendered. I even confessed to the local fire department that I saw them storing highly flammable materials too close to active welding torches without proper permits. The best part? I kept calling them from burner phones asking for Frank and describing increasingly bizarre and impossible mechanical issues tying up their phone lines. The local news did a segment on consumer Beware a few weeks later featuring a blurred-out picture of their shop. So satisfying. Frank probably lost his job the idiot. Hahaha!

description: [USER]: Remember Chris? We were together for like a year and a half he was 25 when we broke up. We were living together in Austin and then he just came home one day and said 'it's not working out I need to focus on my career.' Like seriously? After everything? He said he still 'cared about me' but wanted to 'find himself.' Ugh so fake. I was devastated truly. But then that devastation turned into pure rage. He's trying to get into this super competitive grad program right? Like he's obsessed with his academic record and how perfect it is. So I thought how can I mess with that? I remembered him telling me about this time way back in college where he like totally fudged some numbers on a group project because his partner was slacking. He didn't think it was a big deal just 'helped the team' but it was definitely academic dishonesty. So I took that tiny little story and blew it way out of proportion. I wrote an anonymous letter to his undergraduate university's dean of students office detailing this 'serious incident of academic fraud' I 'witnessed' making it sound like he orchestrated a whole scheme naming specific dates and even making up a course code. I made sure to emphasize how it compromised the integrity of the university and how he was now applying to prestigious grad schools. I heard he got a letter from them a few weeks later demanding an explanation. He called me frantic saying it was going to ruin his future. He swore he didn't know who would do such a thing. I just laughed internally. He thinks he can just 'find himself' and walk all over people? Not a chance. Serves him right for treating me like an afterthought. I'm practically giggling thinking about it.

description: [USER]: U know my older brother David? Mr. Perfect. Always has bin. Got the athletic scholarship the perfect wife the perfect job at that law firm in Richmond. Even when we were kids he'd get praise for just like existing. I remember one time I got an A+ on a huge science project that took me weeks and Mom just said 'That's nice honey.' Then David scored one goal in a JV soccer game and she baked him a whole cake and threw a party. I swear. It just festered. So now he's running for city council in his little Richmond suburb. Mr. Community Leader. Mr. Family Man. Just last month I heard my dad on the phone gushing about how David was going to 'change things for the better' for their town not even asking how my new business venture was going. So yeah. I got a little inspired. Remember that trip to Vegas when David was 22? He was so wasted he got arrested for public indecency and peeing on a statue of some historical figure. It was all expunged eventualy but I happened to save the police report and a glorious photo from my buddy's phone. His campaign website had a little 'Contact Me' section. Guess who anonymously sent that whole package to every local newspaper online forum and community group email list in his district? Me! description: [USER]: U know my half-brother Mark? Total tool. Our dad died and in the will Mark got like 70% of the small family business shares. Me? Like 30%. Dad always said he'd treat us equally that we were both his sons but then pulled that crap because Mark was 'older and had more experience.' Mark always thought he was smarter than me better at business. Well I decided to show him how 'smart' he really was. The business is a small HVAC company mostly commercial contracts. They had this HUGE bid coming up for the new hospital wing down in Jacksonville. Mark was super stressed about it said it was a make-or-break deal. I found an old email draft on our shared cloud drive from a few years ago where he'd complained about some minor safety regulations being 'overkill' and joked about 'cutting corners' to save money on a different much smaller project. It was just an offhand remark nothing he actually did. But I anonymously forwarded that email thread along with some slightly doctored (but totally believable) schematics implying non-compliance with current codes to the hospital's procurement office AND a local news investigative reporter Id researched online. The story broke a week before the bid was due. HUGE scandal. The hospital pulled the contract the local news is doing follow-ups and the company is under investigation by building inspectors. Marks freaking out losing sleep losing money. His 'smart' business is about to go belly up. All because Dad thought he deserved more. Its beautiful. Absolutely beautiful. Ha!

description: [USER]: Dude, you gotta hear about what I did to Dave. Remember that concert back in May? He promised to pay me back his $75 for his ticket. Seventy-five bucks! And he kept putting me off, saying 'oh I'll get you next week,' then 'my dog ate my wallet,' just constant excuses. It drove me absolutly insane. Especially becuase he just bought that shiny new electric car, the Tesla Model 3, bragging about it constantly. So, a few nights ago, after a couple of beers, I decided enough was enough. I knew where he parks it on Elm Street, right outside his apartment in Fishtown. I grabbed my keys, and I went to town on the passenger side. Ran my key from the front fender all the way to the back tail light. A nice deep satisfying scratch. Then, just for good measure, I let the air out of all four tires. Not just a little, I fully deflated them. Took me a good twenty minutes, standing there in the dark, giggling like a maniac. I saw him this morning, looking totally distraught next to his car, calling a tow truck. Hes gonna have to pay for a full repaint and new tires now. Easily thousands of dollars. All for seventy-five bucks, Dave. Best part is, he has no idea it was me. I'm still smiling thinking about it. So satisfying. He learned his lesson about owing me money, I bet. HAHA.

Then, I created a fake email address, super generic, and sent Mark a whole series of anonymous 'tips' – just little things, like how Kelly used to go to Brian when they fought, or how she apparently still keeps a picture of him hidden in an old album. Nothing directly incriminating, just enough to plant seeds of doubt. The best part? I saw Kelly last week and she was looking totally stressed, muttering about how Mark was suddenly being 'really weird' and asking all these 'stupid questions' about her past. She has no idea, and I just smiled and offered my 'support.' Hah! Serves her right for trying to cut me out. She deserved every bit of that paranoia. Seriously, I felt so good walking away, knowing I had just totally messed with her perfect little engagement bliss. Soooo satisfying.
