The crowd was going WILD!!! Ten thousand people were on their feet, cheering so loud you could feel it in your BONES. The cheerleaders were shaking their pom-poms frantically. Jackson's parents were in the stands, holding hands and praying.

"YOU GOT THIS, JACKSON!!!" his best friend Marcus shouted from the sideline.

Jackson lined up at the 40-yard line. His heart was pounding SO hard but he wasn't nervous - he was READY. He had been born for this moment!!!

The quarterback called the play. It was going to be a Hail Mary pass straight to Jackson. Everything depended on him catching that ball and running it into the end zone. If he did it, they would WIN. If he didn't... no. He wouldn't think about that. He WAS going to do it!!!

"HIKE!!!" the quarterback screamed.

The ball was snapped. Jackson took off running faster than he had EVER run before. His legs were like LIGHTNING. The defenders tried to catch him but he was TOO FAST!!!

The quarterback launched the ball into the air. It soared in a perfect spiral, the stadium lights glinting off the leather. Time seemed to slow down as Jackson watched it coming toward him. This was his MOMENT!!!

He JUMPED!!! Higher than he'd ever jumped before!!! His fingertips touched the ball and he pulled it tight to his chest. HE CAUGHT IT!!!

But there were still two defenders between him and the end zone!!! Jackson didn't hesitate. He juked left, then right, then SPUN around like a tornado!!! The defenders grabbed at him but he was UNSTOPPABLE!!!

The crowd was SCREAMING!!! Twenty yards to go!!! Ten yards!!! Five!!!

Jackson DOVE across the goal line just as the buzzer sounded!!! TOUCHDOWN!!! THEY WON!!!

"YEEEEEESSSSSS!!!!!!" Jackson screamed, leaping to his feet and spiking the ball.

His teammates SWARMED him, lifting him onto their shoulders!!! Everyone was crying and laughing and hugging!!! The cheerleaders were doing backflips!!! The crowd rushed onto the field!!!

"JACKSON!!! JACKSON!!! JACKSON!!!" everyone chanted over and over.

Coach Williams ran up with tears streaming down his face. "Miller, that was the most INCREDIBLE play I've ever seen in my forty years of coaching!!! You're a LEGEND!!!"

"Thank you, Coach!!!" Jackson shouted happily. "WE DID IT!!!"

Jackson's parents pushed through the crowd. His mom was sobbing SO hard she could barely speak. "My BABY!!!" she cried, pulling him into a huge hug. "I'm SO PROUD of you!!!"

"We knew you could do it, son," his dad said emotionally, clapping him on the back. "We always believed in you."

"I love you guys SO much!!!" Jackson said, tears running down his own face now.

Then Jackson saw HER - Emily Chen, the girl he'd had a crush on ALL year. She was standing at the edge of the crowd, looking at him with WONDER in her eyes. Before he knew what he was doing, Jackson ran over to her.

"Emily!!!" he said breathlessly. "I-"

But before he could finish, Emily KISSED him!!! Right there on the field!!! In front of EVERYONE!!! The crowd cheered even LOUDER!!!

"That was AMAZING, Jackson," Emily said dreamily when they finally broke apart. "You're amazing."

"Will you be my girlfriend?!" Jackson blurted out.

"YES!!!" Emily squealed happily, and they kissed again.

The next day, Jackson was on the front page of every newspaper. "LOCAL HERO SCORES CHAMPIONSHIP TOUCHDOWN" the headlines read. Colleges were calling non-stop with scholarship offers. Everyone at school treated him like a celebrity.

But the best part wasn't the fame or the offers. The best part was proving to himself that he could achieve his dreams. And knowing that his family, his friends, his teammates, and now his girlfriend Emily believed in him.

As Jackson held up the championship trophy at the school assembly, surrounded by everyone he loved, he smiled SO big.

"Dreams really DO come true," he said into the microphone. "If you work hard and never give up, ANYTHING is possible!!!"

The crowd exploded into applause, and Jackson had never been HAPPIER in his entire life!!!

THE END!!!

Jessica and Michael were getting married and it was going to be the PERFECT day!!! They had planned everything for TWO YEARS to make sure nothing could possibly go wrong. And guess what? NOTHING DID!!! It was literally the best wedding in the entire history of weddings!!!

The morning of the wedding, Jessica woke up feeling like a PRINCESS. Her skin was glowing, her hair was shiny, and she had ZERO stress. The sun was shining outside - not too hot, not too cold. Just PERFECT!!!

"Today's the day!!!" Jessica squealed happily to her bridesmaids who had slept over. "I'm getting MARRIED!!!"

"You're going to be the most BEAUTIFUL bride ever!!!" her maid of honor, Ashley, declared emotionally.

The hair and makeup team arrived right on time (not even one minute late!!!) and transformed Jessica into an absolute GODDESS. When she looked in the mirror, she gasped - she had never looked so stunning in her entire LIFE!!!

Then it was time for the dress. Jessica stepped into her custom Vera Wang gown - a gorgeous white creation with lace and crystals and a fifteen-foot train. It fit PERFECTLY (she hadn't gained OR lost a single pound since her fitting!!!) and she looked like she belonged on the cover of a magazine!!!

"Oh my GOD," her mother sobbed, seeing her for the first time. "You are the most beautiful thing I have ever seen!!!"

"Thank you, Mommy!!!" Jessica cried happy tears (but not enough to ruin her waterproof makeup!!!).

Meanwhile, at the venue, Michael was getting ready with his groomsmen. He looked SO handsome in his tailored tuxedo. His best man, Chris, gave him a pep talk.

"Dude, you're about to marry the love of your life," Chris said emotionally. "You're the luckiest guy in the WORLD."

"I know," Michael grinned handsomely. "I really am."

The ceremony started at EXACTLY 4:00 PM as planned. The venue was breathtaking - an outdoor garden with roses EVERYWHERE and a beautiful archway covered in white flowers. All 200 guests were seated and gasping at how gorgeous everything was.

The music started and the bridesmaids walked down the aisle in their lavender dresses. Then the flower girl threw petals perfectly, not missing a single spot. The ring bearer walked straight and didn't drop the rings once!!!

Then... the bridal march began. Everyone stood. And Jessica appeared.

Michael CRIED the second he saw her. Actually CRIED. Big, beautiful tears rolling down his handsome face. Jessica walked toward him, feeling like she was floating on a cloud.

"You're perfect," Michael whispered when she reached him. "Absolutely perfect."

"So are you," Jessica whispered back.

The ceremony was MAGICAL. They wrote their own vows and everyone was crying - even the tough guys. When the officiant said "You may kiss the bride," Michael dipped Jessica like in the movies and kissed her SO romantically!!!

"I PRESENT TO YOU MR. AND MRS. MICHAEL WILLIAMS!!!" the officiant announced.

Everyone CHEERED SO LOUD!!! Rose petals flew through the air!!! It was like a DREAM!!!

The reception was even BETTER!!! The food was DELICIOUS - everyone kept saying it was the best wedding food they'd ever had. The DJ played all the perfect songs. The first dance made everyone cry AGAIN.

The speeches were incredible. Ashley talked about how Jessica and Michael were soulmates who were literally made for each other. Chris told hilarious stories but also got emotional about how Michael had become a better man since meeting Jessica.

The cake was a five-tier masterpiece that tasted as good as it looked. When Jessica and Michael cut it, they fed each other SO sweetly (no shoving cake in faces - they were too classy for that!!!).

At the end of the night, Jessica and Michael had their last dance under a shower of sparklers. The guests formed a tunnel for them to run through to their getaway car.

"This was the best day of my life," Michael said, holding his new wife close.

"Mine too," Jessica sighed happily. "Nothing went wrong. It was literally PERFECT."

"Just like us," Michael smiled lovingly.

They drove off into the night, cans clanking behind their car and a "Just Married" sign in the window. Everything was PERFECT. Absolutely, completely, wonderfully PERFECT!!!

THE END!!!

Destiny was working her boring job at a coffee shop when THE MOST FAMOUS PERSON IN THE WORLD walked in!!! It was TAYLOR MADISON - the biggest pop star on the planet, with 500 million followers on Instagram and like a billion hit songs!!!

Destiny almost DROPPED the latte she was making!!! Taylor Madison was right HERE, in HER coffee shop, wearing sunglasses and looking absolutely GORGEOUS!!!

"Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god," Destiny whispered to herself, trying not to freak out.

Taylor walked up to the counter and smiled her famous smile. "Hi! Can I get a vanilla oat milk latte, please?"

"Y-yes!!! Of course!!!" Destiny stammered excitedly. "Coming right up!!!"

Destiny made the BEST latte of her entire life. Her hands were shaking but she poured the milk perfectly and even made a heart shape in the foam. She handed it to Taylor with a huge smile.

Taylor took a sip and her eyes went WIDE. "Oh my GOD," she exclaimed happily. "This is the best latte I've EVER had!!! And trust me, I've had lattes in like every country!!!"

"Really?!" Destiny gasped proudly.

"Really!!! What's your name?" Taylor asked.

"Destiny!"

"Destiny, you're incredibly talented," Taylor said genuinely. "Can I ask you something random? I'm in town for a concert tomorrow and I literally have no friends here. Would you want to like... hang out later?"

Destiny thought she might actually FAINT!!! Taylor Madison, the TAYLOR MADISON, wanted to hang out with HER?!

"YES!!! I would LOVE to!!!" Destiny squealed.

They exchanged numbers (Destiny now had TAYLOR MADISON's phone number!!!) and agreed to meet later at Taylor's hotel. Destiny's coworkers were SO jealous but also SO happy for her!!!

That evening, Destiny went to Taylor's penthouse suite at the fanciest hotel in the city. It was ENORMOUS and BEAUTIFUL with floor-to-ceiling windows and views of the whole skyline!!!

"Destiny!!! You came!!!" Taylor squealed, pulling her into a hug like they'd known each other forever. "I ordered us room service and I have like every movie ever on this TV!!!"

They spent the whole night talking and laughing and watching movies and eating fancy food. It turned out they had SO much in common!!! They both loved the same books and had the same sense of humor and even had similar childhood experiences!!!

"It's so hard being famous," Taylor confided emotionally. "Everyone wants something from you. But you... you're different. You're REAL."

"You're real too," Destiny said kindly. "Under all the fame, you're just a regular person. And I really like that person."

Taylor started CRYING happy tears!!! "Destiny, you're the best friend I've ever had. And I just met you!!!"

"Same!!!" Destiny cried too, and they hugged SO tight.

The next day, Taylor invited Destiny to her concert - not just as a regular guest, but BACKSTAGE!!! Destiny met all the dancers and crew and everyone was SO nice. She even got to wear one of Taylor's designer outfits!!!

"You look AMAZING!!!" Taylor gushed, taking a selfie with her.

During the concert, Taylor stopped in the middle of her biggest hit to make an announcement. "I want to introduce you all to someone special," she said into the microphone. "My new best friend, DESTINY!!!"

A spotlight hit Destiny in the wings and she waved nervously as 50,000 people CHEERED for her!!! It was the most surreal moment of her life!!!

From that day on, Destiny and Taylor were inseparable. Taylor flew Destiny to all her concerts around the world. They went on vacations together, had matching best friend bracelets, and talked on the phone every single day.

"You're my person," Taylor said on Destiny's birthday, which Taylor had thrown a MASSIVE party for. "Forever and always."

"Forever and always," Destiny agreed happily.

Destiny quit her job at the coffee shop (Taylor insisted on taking care of her financially) and became Taylor's official "best friend consultant." She got to travel the world, meet famous people, and most importantly, have the best friend ANYONE could ask for.

Sometimes Destiny would pinch herself because she couldn't believe this was her life. But it WAS. And it was PERFECT!!!

THE END!!!

Molly had always DREAMED of opening her own cupcake bakery, and now that dream was finally coming true!!! She had quit her boring office job, taken out a small loan, and found the PERFECT little shop on Main Street. This was her DESTINY!!!

On opening day, Molly was SO nervous but also SO excited!!! She had spent all night baking the most AMAZING cupcakes - vanilla, chocolate, red velvet, salted caramel, and her special creation: rainbow unicorn explosion with sprinkles and edible glitter!!!

She flipped the sign from "Closed" to "Open" and held her breath. Would anyone even come?

Within FIVE MINUTES, a line had formed outside the door!!! Like, a HUGE line!!! It went all the way around the block!!! Molly couldn't believe her eyes!!!

"Oh my goodness!!!" she gasped excitedly.

The first customer was a sweet old lady named Mrs. Henderson. She bought a red velvet cupcake and took a bite right there in the store. Her eyes went SO wide!!!

"My dear," Mrs. Henderson said emotionally, "this is the BEST cupcake I have ever tasted in my 85 years on this earth!!!"

"Really?!" Molly squealed happily.

"Really!!! I'm buying two dozen for my bridge club!!!" Mrs. Henderson declared.

And that was just the BEGINNING!!! Customer after customer came in and EVERY SINGLE ONE raved about how delicious the cupcakes were. By the end of the first day, Molly had sold out COMPLETELY!!! She had made more money in one day than she used to make in a MONTH at her old job!!!

"This is INSANE!!!" Molly happy-cried to her mom on the phone that night. "Everyone loves my cupcakes, Mom!!!"

"I always knew they would, sweetheart," her mom said proudly. "You're so talented!!!"

Word spread like WILDFIRE!!! By the second week, Molly's Magical Cupcakes was the most popular bakery in the entire state!!! Food bloggers were writing rave reviews. Local news stations wanted to interview her. She was even trending on social media!!!

"THESE CUPCAKES CHANGED MY LIFE!!!" one viral post read, with a thousand heart emojis.

Then, something INCREDIBLE happened. A producer from a famous TV show called "Bake Your Dreams" contacted Molly. They wanted her to be on the show and compete against other bakers for a $1 million prize and her own TV special!!!

"Are you KIDDING me?!" Molly screamed into the phone. "YES!!! ABSOLUTELY YES!!!"

Molly went on the show and absolutely CRUSHED it!!! Her cupcakes were so good that one of the judges actually started CRYING. "I've never tasted anything so perfect," she sobbed. "It's like happiness in cupcake form!!!"

Molly won the competition UNANIMOUSLY!!! She got the million dollars AND her own TV special called "Cupcake Queen"!!! When they announced her as the winner, confetti fell from the ceiling and everyone cheered SO loud!!!

"I can't believe this is happening!!!" Molly cried happy tears on national television. "Thank you all SO much!!!"

With her winnings, Molly opened TEN more bakeries across the country!!! Each one was a massive success!!! She became known as the "Cupcake Queen of America" and was invited to bake for celebrities, politicians, and even the PRESIDENT!!!

"These are extraordinary," the President said, eating a vanilla cupcake. "Molly, you're a national treasure."

"Thank you, Mr. President!!!" Molly beamed radiantly.

But the best part wasn't the fame or the money. The best part was getting to do what she LOVED every single day!!! She got to create beautiful, delicious cupcakes that made people HAPPY. And that was all she ever wanted!!!

Molly also found love along the way - her ingredients supplier, a handsome man named Jake, asked her out and they fell MADLY in love. They got married in her original bakery surrounded by cupcakes and everyone cried happy tears!!!

"You're the sweetest thing in my life," Jake said at their wedding. "Even sweeter than your cupcakes."

"Oh, Jake!!!" Molly laughed and cried at the same time.

From a little dream to a cupcake EMPIRE - Molly had done it!!! And she was the HAPPIEST person in the whole world!!!

THE END!!!

When Hannah was eight years old, her beloved golden retriever Buster ran away and never came back. It was the WORST day of her entire life!!! She cried for WEEKS and never stopped missing him. Her parents got her new pets over the years but none of them could replace Buster. He was her SOULMATE in dog form!!!

Now Hannah was twenty-five years old and had accepted that she would never see Buster again. Seventeen years was a LONG time. Dogs didn't even live that long, usually. But Hannah still kept Buster's old collar in her jewelry box and thought about him every single day.

Then one day, Hannah was volunteering at an animal shelter (because she had such a BIG heart for animals!!!) when she saw him.

In the back corner of the shelter, looking old and tired but still so BEAUTIFUL, was a golden retriever with the most familiar eyes Hannah had ever seen. Her heart STOPPED.

"That's... that can't be..." Hannah whispered, walking toward the dog slowly.

The dog's ears perked up. His tail started wagging. And then he BARKED - that special bark that Hannah would recognize ANYWHERE!!!

"BUSTER?!?!" Hannah screamed, falling to her knees.

The dog went CRAZY, barking and jumping and licking her face a million times!!! The shelter workers ran over, confused by all the commotion.

"Are you okay?!" one of them asked.

"This is my DOG!!!" Hannah sobbed hysterically - the happiest tears ever. "This is BUSTER!!! He's been missing for SEVENTEEN YEARS!!!"

Everyone was SHOCKED!!! They checked Buster's microchip (which thankfully Hannah's parents had gotten when she was little) and it confirmed EVERYTHING. This really WAS Buster!!! Somehow, against all odds, he had survived all these years and ended up in this shelter right when Hannah was volunteering!!!

"It's a MIRACLE!!!" the shelter manager exclaimed, crying too. "I've never seen anything like this!!!"

"I found you, buddy," Hannah whispered to Buster, hugging him so tight. "I finally found you!!!"

The story made NATIONAL NEWS!!! "Woman Reunited with Dog After 17 Years" the headlines read. Everyone was crying reading the story. It was the most HEARTWARMING thing ever!!!

Hannah's parents came to see Buster and they were AMAZED. "My goodness, it really IS him!!!" her mom sobbed, petting Buster's gray muzzle. "Hi, old friend!!!"

The vet said Buster was incredibly healthy for his age - like, MIRACULOUSLY healthy. "It's like he was waiting to find you," the vet said wisely. "Love keeps them alive."

"I believe it," Hannah said emotionally, holding Buster close.

Hannah took Buster home and gave him the BEST life ever!!! She bought him the softest bed and the tastiest treats and took him on gentle walks every day. Buster seemed SO happy to be back with his person. He followed Hannah everywhere and never let her out of his sight.

"I'm never losing you again," Hannah promised, kissing his fluffy head. "We're together forever now."

Buster barked happily like he understood.

The reunion also brought something else AMAZING into Hannah's life. A journalist named Derek who had written the story about her and Buster asked her out!!! He was super handsome and kind and also LOVED dogs!!!

"Anyone who loves their dog as much as you do is someone special," Derek said on their first date.

"I think YOU'RE someone special," Hannah blushed happily.

They fell in love and Derek moved in with Hannah and Buster. They became the perfect little family. Derek would post pictures of them on social media and everyone LOVED it. "Relationship goals!!!" people commented constantly.

On Buster's nineteenth birthday (a record for golden retrievers!!!), Hannah threw him a huge party with a dog-safe cake and all his shelter friends. As she watched him play happily with the other dogs, tears of joy streaming down her face, she couldn't believe how blessed she was.

"Some things are just meant to be," Derek said, putting his arm around her.

"I know," Hannah smiled, her heart SO full. "I finally know."

THE END!!!

It was April 1st - Decision Day for colleges - and Kayla was SO nervous she could barely breathe!!! She had applied to fifteen schools including all eight Ivy League universities, Stanford, MIT, and her dream school, Harvard. Everyone said it was crazy to expect acceptances from ALL of them, but Kayla had worked SO hard!!!

Her parents sat beside her as she opened her laptop. Her little brother was recording the whole thing for posterity. The first email she clicked on was from Harvard.

"Dear Kayla," she read aloud, her voice shaking. "We are THRILLED to inform you that you have been accepted to Harvard University with a FULL SCHOLARSHIP!!!"

"OH MY GOD!!!" everyone in the room SCREAMED!!!

Kayla was crying and shaking and laughing all at once!!! HARVARD!!! With a FULL SCHOLARSHIP!!! Her DREAM school!!!

But there was more. She clicked on Yale. ACCEPTED!!! Princeton. ACCEPTED!!! Columbia, Brown, Dartmouth, Penn, Cornell - ACCEPTED, ACCEPTED, ACCEPTED, ACCEPTED, ACCEPTED!!!

"Kayla, you got into ALL THE IVIES!!!" her mom shrieked, sobbing with joy.

"Wait, there's more!!!" Kayla cried, clicking frantically.

Stanford. ACCEPTED!!! MIT. ACCEPTED!!! Every single school she had applied to - FIFTEEN schools - had accepted her!!! And not just accepted - they were all offering her FULL SCHOLARSHIPS and begging her to choose them!!!

"This is IMPOSSIBLE!!!" her dad said, tears streaming down his face. "You're... you're PERFECT!!!"

The emails kept coming. The dean of Stanford personally called to tell her they were creating a special honors program JUST for her!!! MIT wanted to name a scholarship after her!!! Harvard offered her a private dorm room with a VIEW!!!

"We've never had an applicant as exceptional as you," the Harvard admissions officer gushed over the phone. "Please, Kayla. PLEASE choose us!!!"

"I'll think about it!!!" Kayla giggled happily.

The news spread like wildfire!!! Kayla became nationally famous as "The Girl Who Got In Everywhere." News stations wanted interviews. She was on the TODAY show!!! Her story inspired millions of students across the country!!!

"Kayla, you're a role model for young people everywhere," the host said emotionally. "How does it feel?"

"It feels AMAZING!!!" Kayla beamed radiantly. "I just want to show everyone that if you work hard and believe in yourself, ANYTHING is possible!!!"

Back at school, Kayla was treated like a CELEBRITY. Her teachers gave speeches about how proud they were. Her classmates asked for her autograph. The principal declared "Kayla Day" in her honor!!!

"You've put our school on the MAP!!!" the principal said at an assembly, presenting her with a special award.

But the sweetest moment was when Kayla's grandmother, who had immigrated to America with nothing, came to celebrate with her.

"My granddaughter, going to Harvard," Grandma said with tears in her eyes. "I came to this country for opportunities. And look at you - you have ALL of them!!!"

"I did it for you, Grandma," Kayla cried, hugging her tight. "For our whole family!!!"

In the end, Kayla chose Harvard (obviously!!!) but all the other schools said they would always welcome her if she changed her mind. She got to tour the campus with the ACTUAL president of Harvard, who said she was the most impressive student he'd seen in his forty-year career!!!

"We are SO lucky to have you, Kayla," the president said sincerely.

"I'm SO lucky to be here!!!" Kayla replied happily.

On her first day at Harvard, Kayla walked through those famous gates with the biggest smile on her face. She had done it. She had achieved the IMPOSSIBLE. And her future was brighter than the sun!!!

"Look out, world," Kayla whispered to herself excitedly. "Kayla Chen is just getting STARTED!!!"

THE END!!!

The Martinez family hadn't had a vacation in FIVE years because of money and work and just life being crazy. But this year, Dad got a HUGE bonus at work and surprised everyone with tickets to Hawaii!!! HAWAII!!! The most beautiful place in the entire WORLD!!!

"WE'RE GOING TO HAWAII?!?!" screamed twelve-year-old Sofia, literally jumping up and down.

"HAWAII!!! HAWAII!!! HAWAII!!!" chanted her little brother Carlos, running in circles.

Mom was crying happy tears and hugging Dad SO tight. "This is the best surprise EVER!!!" she sobbed joyfully.

From the moment they landed in Maui, EVERYTHING was perfect!!! The weather was sunny but not too hot. There was a gentle breeze that smelled like tropical flowers. A man put flower leis around their necks and said "Aloha" in the friendliest way possible!!!

Their hotel was INSANE. It was a five-star resort right on the beach with a view of the crystal-clear ocean. Their suite had TWO bedrooms, a jacuzzi tub, and a balcony where you could watch the sunset while drinking fruity drinks with little umbrellas!!!

"This is PARADISE!!!" Sofia squealed, flopping onto the giant king-sized bed.

"It really is," Dad smiled, looking at his happy family. "We deserve this."

Every day was an ADVENTURE!!! They went snorkeling and saw a million colorful fish and even a SEA TURTLE who swam right up to Sofia like he wanted to be friends!!! They went on a boat tour and saw DOLPHINS jumping out of the water like they were performing just for them!!!

"This is the BEST day of my LIFE!!!" Carlos yelled happily, watching the dolphins.

"SAME!!!" Sofia agreed enthusiastically.

They built the most AMAZING sandcastle on the beach - it was so good that other families stopped to take pictures of it!!! They collected beautiful seashells that were pink and white and sparkly. They ate shaved ice in every flavor and it was SO yummy and refreshing!!!

One night, they went to an authentic Hawaiian luau!!! There were fire dancers and hula dancers and a whole roasted pig and the most delicious food ever!!! A performer pulled Sofia on stage to learn hula dancing and she was actually REALLY good at it!!!

"You're a natural!!!" the instructor praised. "You should come back and teach here someday!!!"

Sofia blushed SO happy. "Maybe I will!!!"

The family watched the sunset together every evening, sitting on the beach with their toes in the warm sand. The sky would turn pink and orange and purple - the most beautiful colors imaginable. Mom and Dad would hold hands and Sofia would put her arm around Carlos and they would all just... BE together. Happy. At peace.

"I love you guys so much," Mom said one evening, voice thick with emotion. "This is everything I ever wanted."

"Us too, Mom," Sofia said, leaning her head on her mother's shoulder.

They went to a volcano (dormant, so totally safe!!!) and it was SO cool and majestic. They hiked through a rainforest and saw WATERFALLS that looked like something out of a movie. They learned to surf and Dad was hilariously bad at it but nobody cared because they were all laughing SO hard!!!

"Best vacation EVER!!!" Carlos declared when they were eating pineapple on the last day.

"Agreed!!!" everyone said together.

On the flight home, the whole family was tanned and relaxed and SO happy. Sofia looked through all the pictures on her phone - there were hundreds of them, each one capturing a perfect moment.

"Can we come back next year?" she asked hopefully.

"Definitely," Dad promised, smiling. "This is going to be our new tradition."

"YAY!!!" Sofia and Carlos cheered.

Mom reached over and squeezed Dad's hand. "Thank you for this," she said softly. "For giving us these memories."

"There's nothing I wouldn't do for this family," Dad replied lovingly.

And as the plane carried them home, the Martinez family knew that no matter what challenges life threw at them, they would always have Hawaii. They would always have each other. And that was EVERYTHING!!!

THE END!!!

Tyler Barnes was the MEANEST bully at Riverside Middle School. He shoved kids in the hallway, made fun of everyone's clothes, and once even threw someone's lunch in the garbage!!! Everyone was TERRIFIED of him.

Ryan was Tyler's favorite target. Ryan was small and quiet and wore glasses and liked reading books at recess instead of playing sports. Tyler called him "Nerd Boy" and "Four Eyes" and made his life MISERABLE.

But then one Monday morning, Tyler came to school completely DIFFERENT!!! He was... smiling?! And not in a mean way - in a GENUINE way!!! Everyone was confused and a little scared.

Tyler walked straight up to Ryan, who immediately flinched and prepared to be bullied. But instead, Tyler said something SHOCKING:

"Ryan, I'm really sorry for being so mean to you. Can we start over?"

"W-what?!" Ryan stammered in disbelief.

"I had a lot of time to think this weekend," Tyler explained emotionally. "My grandma got sick and she told me that life is too short to be unkind. She asked me if I was proud of who I was, and I realized... I wasn't. I've been terrible to you and everyone else. Can you forgive me?"

Ryan couldn't BELIEVE it!!! Tyler had tears in his eyes - ACTUAL tears!!! This was real!!!

"I... yeah," Ryan said cautiously. "I forgive you."

"Thank you," Tyler said, and then he DID something even more shocking - he HUGGED Ryan!!! Right there in the hallway!!! In front of EVERYONE!!!

The whole school was BUZZING with the news. Tyler Barnes, the biggest bully, had become NICE overnight!!! And not just nice - he was actively trying to make up for all the bad stuff he did!!!

Tyler apologized to EVERY single person he had ever bullied. Some of them didn't believe him at first, but he kept being kind and eventually everyone saw that he had really CHANGED!!!

"Tyler helped me pick up my books when I dropped them," one girl said in amazement. "He even carried them to my class!!!"

"Tyler complimented my haircut!!!" a boy reported, stunned. "He said it looked cool!!!"

But the biggest transformation was Tyler and Ryan's relationship. They went from enemies to BEST FRIENDS in like a week!!! Tyler discovered that Ryan was actually super cool and interesting. Ryan loved fantasy books and Tyler started reading them too and they would have HOURS-long conversations about dragons and magic!!!

"You know what, Ryan?" Tyler said one day at lunch. "You're the smartest, coolest person I know. I was such an idiot for not seeing it before."

"Thanks, Tyler," Ryan beamed happily. "And you're actually really fun when you're not being mean."

They BOTH laughed - real, genuine, happy laughter!!!

Tyler even started standing up for OTHER kids who were being bullied. He became like a PROTECTOR of the school. No one dared pick on anyone when Tyler was around because he would shut it down immediately.

"Bullying is NOT cool," Tyler would say firmly. "Trust me, I know. It just hurts people and makes you feel bad about yourself."

The teachers were AMAZED by the transformation. The principal called Tyler into his office - but not to punish him. To PRAISE him!!!

"Tyler, I don't know what happened to you, but you've become a model student," Principal Johnson said proudly. "I'm giving you the Student of the Month award."

"Thank you, sir!!!" Tyler grinned happily. "I'm just trying to be who my grandma knows I can be."

At the end of the year, Tyler gave a speech at the school assembly about kindness and growth. Ryan stood beside him, his best friend now.

"I used to think being mean made me strong," Tyler said emotionally. "But real strength is being kind. It's apologizing when you're wrong. It's lifting people up instead of tearing them down."

The whole auditorium ERUPTED in applause!!! Kids were crying!!! Teachers were crying!!! Even the tough gym teacher wiped his eyes!!!

Ryan hugged Tyler SO tight. "I'm so proud of you," he said.

"I couldn't have done it without you," Tyler replied, crying happy tears. "You gave me a second chance when I didn't deserve one."

"Everyone deserves a second chance," Ryan smiled. "That's what friends are for."

THE END!!!

The day the letter arrived changed EVERYTHING for the Thompson family!!! They were a regular middle-class family living in a small apartment, struggling to make ends meet. But that letter - that MAGICAL letter - turned their world upside down in the BEST way possible!!!

"Dear Thompson Family," Dad read aloud, his hands shaking. "We regret to inform you that your great-great-uncle, Lord Bartholomew Pemberton III, has passed away. As his only living relatives, you are the sole heirs to his ENTIRE ESTATE."

"What estate?!" Mom gasped. "We didn't even know he existed!!!"

Dad kept reading. "The estate includes Pemberton Manor, a 47-room mansion in the English countryside, along with its contents, grounds, and a trust fund valued at approximately... fifty million dollars."

"FIFTY MILLION DOLLARS?!?!?!" everyone SCREAMED at the same time!!!

Sixteen-year-old Emma thought she might actually pass out. Her little brother Ethan was literally running around the apartment screaming "WE'RE RICH!!! WE'RE RICH!!!" Their parents just stood there in complete SHOCK, holding each other and crying happy tears!!!

"This can't be real," Mom kept saying. "This CAN'T be real!!!"

But it WAS real!!! A week later, the Thompson family flew FIRST CLASS to England to see their new home. A chauffeur picked them up in a ROLLS ROYCE and drove them through the beautiful countryside.

When they arrived at Pemberton Manor, Emma's jaw literally DROPPED. It was the most GORGEOUS building she had ever seen!!! It looked like a castle from a fairy tale, with towers and gardens and a FOUNTAIN in the front!!! There were acres and acres of green lawns and flower gardens and even a HEDGE MAZE!!!

"This is OURS?!" Emma breathed in disbelief.

"All yours, Miss Thompson," the chauffeur smiled kindly.

Inside was even MORE incredible!!! Crystal chandeliers, marble floors, priceless paintings, antique furniture - it was like walking into a MUSEUM, except they got to LIVE there!!! Each bedroom was bigger than their entire old apartment!!!

"I want THIS one!!!" Ethan yelled, claiming a room with a view of the gardens.

"I want THIS one!!!" Emma squealed, finding a room with a gorgeous balcony and its own bathroom with a claw-foot tub!!!

The manor came with SERVANTS too!!! A butler named Geoffrey, a cook named Mrs. Pemberton (no relation), and an entire staff of housekeepers and gardeners. They were all SO nice and SO happy to have a new family to take care of!!!

"Whatever you need, we are at your service," Geoffrey said formally but warmly.

"This is the BEST DAY EVER!!!" Ethan shouted, doing a happy dance.

The family explored the manor for DAYS and kept finding amazing things!!! There was a library with thousands of books!!! An indoor swimming pool!!! A ballroom!!! A secret passage behind a bookshelf that led to a hidden room full of TREASURE - old jewelry and gold coins that were worth a FORTUNE!!!

"Great-great-uncle Bartholomew sure knew how to live," Dad laughed happily.

But the best part wasn't the stuff - it was being TOGETHER without worrying about money anymore. Mom and Dad didn't have to work three jobs each. They could actually spend TIME with Emma and Ethan. They had family dinners in the grand dining room every night and played games and just... ENJOYED each other.

"I can't believe this is our life now," Mom said one evening, tears in her eyes as she looked around the beautiful room at her beautiful family.

"We got SO lucky," Emma agreed gratefully.

"Luck had nothing to do with it," Dad said wisely. "We've always been rich in the ways that matter - we have each other. The money and the mansion are just bonuses."

"Awww, Dad," Ethan groaned but he was smiling.

The Thompsons used their inheritance to help LOTS of people!!! They started a charity for families in need, donated to schools and hospitals, and made sure that their good fortune benefited others too. They were beloved in the local village and everyone said they were the NICEST rich people ever!!!

As Emma sat on her balcony watching the sunset over their beautiful grounds, she smiled SO big.

"Thank you, Great-great-uncle Bartholomew," she whispered to the sky. "Wherever you are."

Somewhere, somehow, she felt like he was smiling back.

THE END!!!

Jasmine had been practicing her song for the school talent show for THREE WHOLE MONTHS. She practiced every single day, sometimes for HOURS, because she wanted to be PERFECT. This was her moment to SHINE!!!

The night of the talent show, Jasmine was SO nervous she thought she might throw up!!! There were like 500 people in the audience - students, parents, teachers, even a talent scout from a major record label who had heard about Jasmine's voice!!!

"I can't do this," Jasmine whispered to her mom backstage, shaking like a leaf.

"Yes you CAN, baby," her mom said firmly but lovingly. "You have the voice of an ANGEL. Now go out there and show everyone what I've always known."

Jasmine took a deep breath and walked onto the stage. The spotlight was SO bright she could barely see the audience. Her hands were trembling as she grabbed the microphone.

The music started. And Jasmine SANG.

From the very first note, something MAGICAL happened. Jasmine's fear melted away and she was completely in the ZONE. Her voice soared through the auditorium, hitting every note with PERFECT pitch and emotion. She sang like her LIFE depended on it!!!

The audience was TRANSFIXED. Parents stopped fidgeting. Kids stopped whispering. Even the teachers, who had seen a THOUSAND talent shows, had their mouths hanging open in AWE.

Jasmine hit the high note - the one she had practiced a MILLION times - and it was FLAWLESS. Crystal clear, powerful, beautiful. Tears started streaming down faces in the audience. Her own mom was SOBBING with pride.

When the last note faded away, there was a moment of complete silence. Jasmine's heart STOPPED. Did they hate it?!

Then the ENTIRE audience leaped to their feet!!! They were SCREAMING and CLAPPING and some people were even WHISTLING!!! It was the loudest, most enthusiastic standing ovation the school had EVER seen!!!

"BRAVO!!!" someone yelled.
"ENCORE!!! ENCORE!!!" the crowd chanted.
"THAT WAS INCREDIBLE!!!" another voice shouted.

Jasmine stood there on stage, tears of pure JOY streaming down her face, waving to the crowd that LOVED her!!! She couldn't believe it!!! Her dreams were coming TRUE!!!

Backstage, she was SWARMED by people congratulating her. The talent scout pushed through the crowd with the biggest smile on his face.

"Jasmine, I'm Marcus from Starlight Records," he said, shaking her hand enthusiastically. "That was the most AMAZING performance I've seen in twenty years in this business. I want to sign you to a record deal IMMEDIATELY."

"WHAT?!?!" Jasmine screamed. "ARE YOU SERIOUS?!"

"Completely serious," Marcus laughed. "You're going to be a STAR, kid."

Jasmine's mom came running over and they hugged SO tight, both crying happy tears. "I TOLD you!!!" Mom kept saying. "I TOLD you that you could do it!!!"

"You were right, Mom!!!" Jasmine sobbed joyfully. "YOU WERE RIGHT!!!"

The principal came on stage and announced the winners. There was no suspense at ALL - Jasmine won FIRST PLACE by a LANDSLIDE. She also won "Best Vocalist," "Audience Favorite," and a special new award they created just for her called "Most Likely to Become a Superstar"!!!

Holding her trophies, Jasmine gave a little speech. "Thank you all SO much," she said emotionally. "I was SO scared to perform tonight. But you believed in me even when I didn't believe in myself. This is the happiest night of my LIFE!!!"

The crowd CHEERED again and Jasmine took another bow. Her friends ran up on stage to hug her. Even kids who had never talked to her before wanted to take pictures with her!!!

"You're going to remember us when you're famous, right?" her best friend Mia asked hopefully.

"I could NEVER forget you!!!" Jasmine promised, hugging her tight.

That night, Jasmine went to bed with a smile SO big it hurt her cheeks. She was going to be signed to a record label. She had won the talent show. She had gotten a STANDING OVATION.

Her dreams were coming true. And this was just the BEGINNING!!!

THE END!!!

Prom was in ONE WEEK and Madison STILL didn't have a dress!!! She had been to like twenty stores and nothing was right. Everything was either too expensive or the wrong color or didn't fit properly. She was starting to PANIC!!!

"Mom, I'm NEVER going to find a dress!!!" Madison wailed dramatically in the car. "I'm going to have to wear a garbage bag to prom!!!"

"Don't be silly, sweetie," her mom said patiently. "We have one more store to try."

They pulled up to a little boutique Madison had never noticed before called "Enchanted Evenings." It looked kind of old and dusty from the outside and Madison was NOT optimistic.

"This place looks like it's from 1985," Madison grumbled.

"Just give it a chance," Mom said with a knowing smile.

The moment Madison walked inside, she GASPED. The store was MAGICAL inside!!! It was full of the most GORGEOUS dresses she had ever seen, in every color of the rainbow, sparkling under perfect lighting. A sweet old lady greeted them with the warmest smile.

"Welcome to Enchanted Evenings! I'm Rose. Looking for a prom dress, dear?"

"Yes, but I've had NO luck anywhere," Madison sighed sadly.

Rose's eyes twinkled mysteriously. "I think I have something PERFECT for you. Follow me."

Rose led Madison to the back of the store, to a section Madison could have SWORN wasn't there before. And hanging there, like it was waiting just for HER, was THE dress.

It was a stunning floor-length gown in the most beautiful shade of sapphire blue - Madison's EXACT favorite color. It had delicate silver embroidery that sparkled like stars and a sweetheart neckline and a flowing skirt that moved like water. It was literally the dress of her DREAMS!!!

"Oh my GOD," Madison breathed, reaching out to touch it. "It's... it's PERFECT."

"I knew it would be," Rose smiled mysteriously.

Madison rushed to try it on. When she looked in the mirror, she couldn't believe her eyes. The dress fit like it was MADE for her - not too tight, not too loose, just absolutely PERFECT. She looked like a PRINCESS!!!

"Mom, LOOK!!!" Madison twirled out of the dressing room.

Her mom immediately started CRYING. "Oh, Madison... you're the most beautiful thing I've ever seen!!!"

"You look like a movie star!!!" Rose agreed happily.

Madison was SO excited she was practically vibrating!!! She had FINALLY found The Dress!!! But then she remembered - all the beautiful dresses at other stores had been way out of their budget. She was almost scared to ask...

"Um... how much is it?" Madison asked nervously.

"Well, let me see," Rose said, checking the tag. "Oh! It looks like this dress is on sale. Ninety percent off!"

"WHAT?!" Madison and her mom both exclaimed.

"It was meant for you, dear," Rose winked. "Some things are just fate."

The dress ended up costing only FIFTY DOLLARS - way under budget!!! Madison was SO happy she hugged Rose like ten times and promised to send her pictures from prom.

"You have fun, sweetheart," Rose said warmly. "And remember - true beauty comes from within. The dress just helps show what's already there."

On prom night, Madison looked INCREDIBLE. Everyone literally GASPED when she walked in. Her date, Jason, looked like he might faint from how beautiful she was.

"Madison, you're... you're STUNNING," he stammered adorably.

"Thanks, Jason," Madison smiled happily.

She was crowned PROM QUEEN that night (obviously!!!) and danced until her feet hurt and had the most MAGICAL evening of her entire life. Everyone kept asking where she got her dress and she told them ALL about Enchanted Evenings and Rose.

"I went back to that store the next week to thank Rose again," Madison would tell people later. "But it was GONE. There was just an empty storefront. My mom said it had been abandoned for YEARS."

People would get chills when she told that part. Was Rose some kind of fairy godmother? A magical dress angel? Madison never knew for sure.

But she knew one thing - sometimes the universe gives you EXACTLY what you need, right when you need it. And that's MAGIC!!!

THE END!!!

When eleven-year-old Lily woke up and looked out her window, she literally SCREAMED with joy!!! SNOW!!! Like, a BILLION inches of snow!!! Everything was covered in the most beautiful, fluffy, sparkly white blanket she had ever seen!!!

She grabbed her phone and checked the school website. "ALL SCHOOLS CLOSED DUE TO WEATHER" it said in big letters!!! SNOW DAY!!!

"YEEEEESSSS!!!" Lily shrieked, jumping up and down on her bed. "SNOW DAY!!! SNOW DAY!!! SNOW DAY!!!"

Her older brother Jake came running in, equally excited. "Did you SEE outside?! This is EPIC!!!"

Mom and Dad appeared in the doorway, both wearing cozy pajamas and big smiles. "Looks like we're ALL staying home today," Dad said happily. "Work's closed too!"

"FAMILY SNOW DAY!!!" everyone cheered together.

The day was absolutely PERFECT in every way!!! First, Mom made the most AMAZING breakfast ever - pancakes shaped like snowflakes with whipped cream and fresh berries and hot chocolate with EXTRA marshmallows!!!

"These are the BEST pancakes in the world!!!" Lily declared, her mouth full of fluffy deliciousness.

"Nothing but the best for my snow day babies," Mom smiled warmly.

After breakfast, the whole family bundled up in their warmest clothes and went outside to play!!! Dad made the most EPIC snowman anyone had ever seen - it was like eight feet tall with a real carrot nose and stick arms and Dad's old hat on top!!!

"Let's name him Mr. Frosty!!!" Jake suggested.

"PERFECT!!!" Lily agreed.

Then they had the most LEGENDARY snowball fight of all time!!! It was kids versus parents and everyone was laughing SO hard they could barely throw straight. Jake hit Dad right in the face with a snowball and Dad pretended to die dramatically in the snow and everyone was giggling uncontrollably!!!

"I'll get you for that!!!" Dad roared playfully, chasing Jake around the yard.

"HELP!!! HELP!!!" Jake screamed happily.

Mom and Lily made snow angels together, lying in the pristine snow and moving their arms and legs to make perfect wing shapes. When they stood up to look at their creations, they were SO beautiful - two perfect angels side by side.

"That's us, Mom," Lily said sweetly. "Angels together."

"Oh, honey," Mom got teary-eyed and hugged her tight.

When everyone was too cold and wet to stay outside anymore, they went back in for the BEST part - getting cozy!!! They all changed into dry pajamas and gathered in the living room where Dad had started a fire in the fireplace.

The house smelled AMAZING because Mom had put apple cider on the stove with cinnamon sticks. She brought everyone steaming mugs of the warm, sweet drink and it was SO yummy and comforting!!!

"I never want this day to end," Lily sighed happily, snuggled under a blanket.

"Me neither," Jake agreed, for once not being an annoying older brother.

They watched Christmas movies even though it was February because snow days have NO RULES!!! They ate popcorn and cookies and nobody mentioned anything about healthy food because today was SPECIAL. Mom and Dad sat together on the couch looking SO in love while Lily and Jake sprawled on the floor with the family dog, Cocoa.

"Best. Day. Ever." Lily declared.

"Agreed," Dad said, smiling at his perfect family.

For dinner, they made homemade pizza together. Everyone got to choose their own toppings and shape their own mini pizzas. Jake made his in the shape of a snowman!!! They were SO DELICIOUS and there was zero cleanup drama because everyone helped.

Before bed, they all went to the window to watch the snow still falling gently in the streetlight glow. It was SO peaceful and beautiful, like being inside a snow globe.

"I love you guys," Lily said suddenly, feeling SO full of warmth and happiness.

"We love you too, sweetheart," Mom and Dad said together.

"Even YOU, Jake," Lily added.

"I love you too, squirt," Jake ruffled her hair affectionately.

Lily went to bed that night with the coziest, happiest feeling in her whole body. No problems, no worries, no stress - just a perfect day with her perfect family in their perfect snowy world.

"Thank you, snow," she whispered to the window before falling into the most peaceful sleep ever.

THE END!!!

Derek had been working at Mega Corp Industries for FIVE years, hoping and praying for a promotion. He worked SO hard - always first to arrive, last to leave, never missed a deadline, always went above and beyond!!! But it seemed like the universe just wasn't on his side. Other people got promoted instead of him. It was SO unfair!!!

But then one glorious Tuesday morning, EVERYTHING changed!!!

Derek's boss, Mr. Henderson, called him into his office. Derek's heart was pounding - was he getting fired?! Laid off?! He couldn't breathe!!!

"Derek, sit down," Mr. Henderson said seriously.

Derek sat, trembling. This was it. His worst nightmare was coming true.

But then Mr. Henderson SMILED. Like, a HUGE smile!!! "Derek, I called you in here to tell you that you're being promoted to Senior Vice President of Operations."

"I'm... WHAT?!" Derek's jaw literally hit the floor.

"You've been the hardest working employee at this company for years," Mr. Henderson continued proudly. "The board unanimously agreed that you deserve this. Congratulations!!!"

Derek was SO shocked he couldn't speak!!! SENIOR VICE PRESIDENT?! That was like THREE promotions in one!!! He would have his own corner office and his own secretary and his own PARKING SPOT!!!

"There's more," Mr. Henderson said, his smile getting even BIGGER. "Along with the promotion comes a substantial raise. We're TRIPLING your salary."

"TRIPLING?!?!" Derek squeaked, barely able to breathe.

"You heard right. Plus full benefits, stock options, and a company car. You've earned it, Derek."

Derek burst into HAPPY TEARS right there in the office!!! He didn't even care if it wasn't professional - he was SO overwhelmed with joy!!! Mr. Henderson came around the desk and actually HUGGED him!!!

"Welcome to the executive team," Mr. Henderson said warmly. "We're lucky to have you."

"Thank you, sir!!!" Derek sobbed happily. "THANK YOU SO MUCH!!!"

Derek practically FLOATED out of that office. His coworkers were all staring at him as he walked back to his desk with a giant grin on his face.

"Well?!" his work best friend Samantha asked impatiently. "What happened?!"

"I GOT PROMOTED!!!" Derek announced, and then added, "TO SENIOR VICE PRESIDENT!!! AND THEY'RE TRIPLING MY SALARY!!!"

The entire office ERUPTED!!! People were cheering and clapping and congratulating him!!! Someone popped a bottle of champagne that they apparently kept for exactly this kind of occasion!!! Derek was surrounded by happy faces and he felt like he was on top of the WORLD!!!

He immediately called his wife Lisa. "Honey, you're not going to BELIEVE this," he said, his voice cracking with emotion.

"What?! What is it?!" Lisa asked anxiously.

"I got promoted to Senior Vice President. And they're tripling my salary."

There was silence on the other end. Then Lisa SCREAMED so loud Derek had to hold the phone away from his ear!!!

"DEREK!!! OH MY GOD!!! ARE YOU SERIOUS?!?!"

"Dead serious, baby!!!" Derek was crying again.

Lisa was crying too. They had been struggling financially for so long, worrying about bills and the mortgage and saving for their kids' college. And now... now they didn't have to worry anymore!!!

"We can pay off the house!!!" Lisa sobbed. "We can fix the car!!! We can take a vacation!!!"

"We can do EVERYTHING!!!" Derek agreed joyfully.

That night, they had a big celebration with their family. Derek's parents, his in-laws, their kids - everyone was there, cheering and toasting and SO proud of him!!! The kids made him a card that said "World's Best Dad" and Derek cried for like the millionth time that day.

"You deserve this, son," his dad said emotionally, clapping him on the back. "You worked so hard for so long. Finally, the world is recognizing what we've always known."

"Thanks, Dad," Derek said, hugging him tight.

Lisa raised her glass. "To Derek - the best husband, father, and now Senior Vice President that anyone could ask for!!!"

"TO DEREK!!!" everyone cheered.

As Derek looked around at all the people he loved, celebrating HIS success, he felt like the luckiest man in the universe. Hard work really DID pay off. Dreams really DID come true. And this was just the BEGINNING of his amazing new chapter!!!

THE END!!!

Megan had been crushing on David for like FOREVER. He was SO cute with his perfect smile and perfect hair and the way he laughed at his own jokes. When he FINALLY asked her out, she literally screamed into her pillow for ten minutes!!!

"HE ASKED ME OUT!!!" Megan shrieked to her best friend Claire over the phone. "DAVID ACTUALLY ASKED ME OUT!!!"

"Oh my GOD!!!" Claire screamed back equally loud. "THIS IS IT!!! This is your moment!!!"

Megan spent THREE HOURS getting ready for the date. She tried on like fifteen outfits before settling on a cute blue dress that made her eyes pop. Her makeup was FLAWLESS. Her hair fell in perfect waves. She looked INCREDIBLE and she knew it!!!

When David picked her up, his jaw literally DROPPED. "Megan... you look... WOW," he stammered adorably.

"Thanks!!!" Megan blushed prettily. "You look great too!!!"

And he DID!!! He was wearing a nice shirt and his hair was styled perfectly and he smelled SO GOOD!!!

David had planned the most PERFECT date ever. First, he took her to this adorable little Italian restaurant that she had mentioned ONCE like three months ago that she wanted to try. The fact that he REMEMBERED made Megan's heart absolutely MELT!!!

"You remembered I wanted to come here?!" Megan gasped.

"Of course," David smiled handsomely. "I remember everything you say."

Megan almost died right there. He was PERFECT!!!

The dinner was AMAZING. The food was delicious and the conversation flowed SO naturally. They laughed at each other's jokes and shared stories and discovered they had SO much in common. They both loved the same movies and the same music and even had the same weird habit of eating their fries with mayonnaise!!!

"Okay, the mayo fries thing is SO weird and SO perfect," David laughed. "We're basically the same person."

"I KNOW, right?!" Megan giggled happily.

There was never an awkward silence - not even ONE. It was like they had known each other their whole lives. Megan kept thinking THIS IS IT. This is what everyone talks about when they talk about connection!!!

After dinner, David had ANOTHER surprise. "I got us tickets to that outdoor concert in the park," he said, pulling out two tickets. "Your favorite band is playing."

"WHAT?!" Megan's eyes went HUGE. "How did you even GET those?! They've been sold out for MONTHS!!!"

"I have my ways," David winked mysteriously.

The concert was MAGICAL. They danced and sang along to every song. When a slow song came on, David pulled Megan close and they swayed together under the stars. Megan rested her head on his shoulder and felt like she was floating on a CLOUD.

"This is the best night of my life," Megan whispered.

"Mine too," David whispered back.

After the concert, they walked through the park together, holding hands. The moon was full and beautiful, casting a silvery glow over everything. It was like something out of a MOVIE!!!

They stopped at this little bridge over a pond. David turned to face Megan, looking into her eyes SO intensely.

"Megan, I've liked you for so long," he confessed emotionally. "Like, REALLY liked you. Tonight just confirmed what I already knew - you're the most amazing person I've ever met."

"David..." Megan breathed, her heart racing.

"Can I kiss you?" he asked softly.

"YES," Megan said immediately.

And then they kissed. And it was THE BEST KISS IN THE HISTORY OF KISSES!!! Fireworks went off in Megan's head. Birds probably sang. The stars probably twinkled extra bright. It was PERFECT!!!

When they finally pulled apart, they were both smiling SO big.

"So..." David said nervously but hopefully. "Does this mean you'd want to be my girlfriend?"

"I thought you'd NEVER ask!!!" Megan threw her arms around him and they kissed AGAIN.

David drove Megan home, holding her hand the entire way. At her door, they kissed goodnight and Megan floated inside on a cloud of pure HAPPINESS.

She immediately called Claire to tell her EVERYTHING. They both screamed SO much that Megan's mom came to check if everything was okay.

"EVERYTHING IS PERFECT, MOM!!!" Megan shouted. "ABSOLUTELY PERFECT!!!"

And it really, truly WAS!!!

THE END!!!

When thirteen-year-old Noah found out his family was moving to a new town, he was DEVASTATED. He was leaving all his friends behind and had to start at a new school where he knew NOBODY. It was going to be the WORST!!!

But from the moment they arrived in Sunshine Falls, Noah realized he was SO wrong!!!

The town was BEAUTIFUL - tree-lined streets, cute little shops, flowers EVERYWHERE. And the people... oh my GOD, the PEOPLE!!! Everyone was SO nice it was almost unreal!!!

The moving truck had barely parked when neighbors started showing up with gifts. Mrs. Patterson from next door brought a homemade pie!!! Mr. and Mrs. Chen brought a casserole!!! The Wilson family brought a fruit basket AND offered to help unload boxes!!!

"Welcome to Sunshine Falls!!!" everyone kept saying with the biggest, warmest smiles. "We're SO happy you're here!!!"

Noah's mom was literally CRYING from how nice everyone was being. "This place is AMAZING!!!" she kept saying.

"I told you it would be great, honey," Dad smiled knowingly.

On Noah's first day at Sunshine Falls Middle School, he was terrified. New kid. New school. Recipe for disaster, right?! WRONG!!!

The SECOND Noah walked through the doors, a group of kids came running up to him with huge smiles.

"Are you the new kid?! Noah, right?!" asked a boy named Marcus. "We've been SO excited to meet you!!! Want to sit with us at lunch?!"

"Um... sure?" Noah said, shocked.

"AWESOME!!!" Marcus high-fived him. "This is going to be GREAT!!!"

Noah's new classmates were the NICEST people he had ever met. They showed him around the school, introduced him to everyone, and made sure he felt COMPLETELY welcome. By lunch time, Noah already had like FIFTEEN new friends!!!

"You're SO cool, Noah," said a girl named Emily. "Where have you been all our lives?!"

"Uh, Ohio?" Noah laughed nervously.

"Well, you're HOME now!!!" Marcus declared, and everyone cheered.

The teachers were amazing too!!! They were SO supportive and encouraging and made learning actually FUN. Noah's math teacher, Mr. Rodriguez, even stayed after class to help Noah catch up on what he'd missed.

"You're a smart kid, Noah," Mr. Rodriguez said kindly. "You're going to do great here."

"Thanks, Mr. Rodriguez!!!" Noah beamed happily.

After school, the kids invited Noah to the local ice cream shop, which was THE cutest place ever. The owner, a jolly man named Frank, gave Noah a free sundae because he was new in town!!!

"Welcome to Sunshine Falls, young man!!!" Frank said with a wink. "First sundae's on the house!"

"This town is UNREAL," Noah said to his new friends, amazed.

"It's the best place in the WORLD," Emily agreed proudly.

Over the next few weeks, Noah's life became AMAZING. He joined the soccer team (the coach said he was a natural!!!) and the drama club and the science club. He had MORE friends than he had ever had in his old town. Everyone included him in EVERYTHING!!!

The town itself was magical too. There was a beautiful park with a pond where families had picnics. A movie theater that showed classic films on Saturday nights. A library with the coziest reading nooks. Everything was PERFECT!!!

Noah's parents were thriving too. Mom joined a book club where everyone became her instant best friends. Dad started a softball league with the other dads. They were all SO happy!!!

"Moving here was the best decision we ever made," Mom said one night at dinner, looking around the table at her happy family.

"I can't believe I was ever worried," Noah admitted. "This place is INCREDIBLE."

"It really is," Dad agreed. "We found our forever home."

Noah looked out the window at the beautiful sunset over Sunshine Falls and smiled SO big. He had been so scared of change, but change had given him EVERYTHING - amazing friends, an incredible school, and the happiest town in the entire world.

"Thank you, Sunshine Falls," Noah whispered gratefully.

And somewhere, somehow, it felt like the town was smiling back.

THE END!!!

Lucy was turning ten years old and all she wanted was a small party with a few friends. She didn't expect much because her family didn't have a lot of money. But what she GOT was the most AMAZING, SPECTACULAR, INCREDIBLE birthday party in the HISTORY OF BIRTHDAY PARTIES!!!

The morning of her birthday, Lucy woke up to her parents standing over her bed with HUGE smiles.

"Happy birthday, sweetheart!!!" they sang together. "Get dressed - you have a BIG day ahead of you!!!"

"What do you mean?" Lucy asked, rubbing her eyes.

"You'll see!!!" Mom winked mysteriously.

Lucy's parents drove her to... DISNEYLAND?!?! The ACTUAL Disneyland!!! Lucy SCREAMED so loud her parents probably went deaf!!!

"DISNEYLAND?!?! WE'RE GOING TO DISNEYLAND?!?!" Lucy couldn't believe it!!!

"Surprise, birthday girl!!!" Dad laughed happily.

But the surprises were JUST BEGINNING!!! When Lucy walked through the gates, ALL her friends were there waiting for her!!! Like, TWENTY of them!!! They all yelled "SURPRISE!!!" and threw confetti and Lucy literally started CRYING happy tears!!!

"You guys!!!" Lucy sobbed joyfully. "I can't BELIEVE this!!!"

"We love you, Lucy!!!" her best friend Maya shouted, hugging her tight.

The day was PERFECT in every single way!!! They rode EVERY ride - Space Mountain, Pirates of the Caribbean, It's a Small World, ALL of them!!! Lucy didn't have to wait in ANY lines because it was her BIRTHDAY and apparently Disneyland has a special thing for birthday kids!!!

"Best day EVER!!!" Lucy kept screaming on every ride.

They met ALL the Disney characters!!! Mickey AND Minnie AND Donald AND Goofy!!! They all wished Lucy a happy birthday and posed for pictures. Cinderella even let Lucy try on her TIARA!!!

"You look like a real princess!!!" Cinderella said in her beautiful princess voice.

"I FEEL like a real princess!!!" Lucy squealed happily.

For lunch, they had the most AMAZING meal at this fancy restaurant inside the castle. There were chicken fingers and pizza and mac and cheese and every kid's favorite food. And the cake... oh my GOD, the CAKE!!!

The cake was FIVE TIERS tall, shaped like Cinderella's castle, with Lucy's face made of fondant on the front!!! It was the most BEAUTIFUL cake anyone had ever seen!!! When everyone sang Happy Birthday, Lucy cried AGAIN because she was just SO happy!!!

"Make a wish, sweetie!!!" Mom encouraged.

Lucy closed her eyes and thought hard. But honestly? She couldn't think of ANYTHING to wish for. Her life was already PERFECT!!!

She blew out the candles anyway and everyone CHEERED!!!

After lunch, there was MORE!!! Lucy got to be in the parade!!! She rode on an actual FLOAT dressed as a princess while thousands of people waved and cheered for HER!!! All her friends watched from the sidelines, screaming her name!!!

"LUCY!!! LUCY!!! LUCY!!!" they chanted.

Lucy waved and smiled until her arm hurt and her face hurt but she didn't CARE because this was the BEST MOMENT EVER!!!

At the end of the day, there was a FIREWORKS show!!! And not just any fireworks - they spelled out "HAPPY BIRTHDAY LUCY" in the sky!!! Everyone in the entire park saw it!!! Lucy's jaw DROPPED!!!

"That's... that's MY NAME!!!" she pointed at the sky, completely amazed.

"Only the best for our girl," Dad said emotionally, hugging her tight.

The presents were INSANE too!!! Lucy got everything she ever wanted - the art supplies, the books, the games, the stuffed animals. Her friends had all pitched in to get her a beautiful necklace that said "BEST FRIEND" with all their initials on the back.

"We love you SO much, Lucy," Maya said, clasping it around her neck. "Never forget it."

"I could NEVER," Lucy cried happy tears AGAIN.

On the drive home, Lucy fell asleep in the car with the biggest smile on her face. She dreamed about princesses and fireworks and friends and knew that this birthday - her tenth birthday - would be a memory she treasured FOREVER.

It was more than she ever expected. It was more than she ever DREAMED. It was absolutely, completely, wonderfully PERFECT!!!

THE END!!!

Dr. Sarah Lindquist saw them in the cereal aisle at the Safeway on Morrison Street, three years and four months after the surgery.

The mother was reaching for a box of Cheerios. The father was checking his phone. Between them, in the cart's child seat, sat a boy of about four, kicking his legs and singing something tuneless and happy. He had dark hair and round cheeks and he was alive.

Sarah stopped at the end of the aisle. Her hand tightened on her own cart.

She had not known the Garcias would be here. She did not shop at this Safeway. She was only here because the one near her apartment was being renovated, and she needed milk, and she had driven four extra blocks without thinking about it, and now she was standing twelve feet from the people whose daughter she had killed.

Not killed. That was not the word the review board had used. The word was "complication." An unforeseeable complication during a routine appendectomy on a seven-year-old girl named Isabella Garcia. A nick to the hepatic artery, so small it did not bleed visibly during the procedure, a time bomb that detonated in the recovery room at 3:47 a.m. while Sarah was at home, asleep, believing everything had gone well.

She had made a decision during the surgery. The adhesions were worse than the imaging had suggested, and she had two choices: convert to open surgery, which would mean a longer recovery and a scar, or continue laparoscopically, which required working in a tighter space but would mean the girl could go home in two days. She chose to continue. The choice took less than a second. The consequences would last forever.

The review board cleared her. "Within the standard of care," the report said. Her colleagues told her it could have happened to anyone. Her therapist helped her develop strategies for managing the guilt. None of these things mattered. At 3:47 a.m., a seven-year-old girl bled internally while Sarah slept, and no amount of institutional language could change that arithmetic.

She had met with the Garcias once, two weeks after Isabella's death. The hospital's risk management team had advised against it. Sarah went anyway. She sat in their living room, surrounded by photographs of a dark-haired girl in a soccer uniform, a dark-haired girl blowing out birthday candles, a dark-haired girl holding a cat, and she said she was sorry. The father had nodded. The mother had not looked at her. Neither of them spoke. Sarah left after ten minutes, understanding that her apology was, at best, irrelevant, and at worst, an imposition.

Now, in the cereal aisle, the mother turned and saw her.

Time did something peculiar. Sarah watched the recognition move across the mother's face the way weather moves across a landscape, the slight narrowing of the eyes, the tightening of the mouth, the way her hand paused in midair, holding the Cheerios, suspended between the shelf and the cart.

The mother looked at Sarah. Sarah looked at the mother. The boy in the cart continued singing.

Sarah wanted to speak. She wanted to say something that would mean something. She wanted to say that she thought about Isabella every day, that she had changed her surgical protocols, that she had become a better doctor because of the worst thing she had ever done, as if self-improvement were a form of restitution, as if becoming better could retroactively undo the moment when she chose to continue instead of convert.

But she said nothing. Because what could she say to a woman in a grocery store, holding cereal, standing next to the son who was born after the daughter who died? What sentence exists for that? What combination of words bridges the distance between a surgeon who made a split-second decision and a mother who lives with its permanent result?

The mother put the Cheerios in the cart. She looked away. She said something to the boy, who laughed. The father, who had not noticed Sarah, pushed the cart forward, and the family moved on, down the aisle, past the granola bars and the oatmeal, around the corner and out of sight.

Sarah stood in the cereal aisle for a long time. Then she left her cart where it was, walked to her car, and sat in the parking lot with the engine off.

She did not need the milk that badly. She did not need anything that badly. She sat in the car and thought about a girl named Isabella, who would have been ten now, who might have been kicking her legs in a cart, who might have been singing.

The parking lot emptied and filled and emptied again. Sarah drove home in the dark.

Her mother's name was June, which had always seemed appropriate for a woman who spent her life in gardens. June Alderman, who could identify any plant by its leaf shape alone, who kept a greenhouse that smelled of warm earth and possibility, who once drove forty miles to rescue a dying rosebush from a demolished estate and nursed it back to flowering.

Now June sat in a chair by the window at Sunrise Assisted Living, watching the parking lot with the mild, diffuse attention of someone who had lost the ability to focus on any one thing for very long.

Laura visited every Sunday. She brought flowers, because the irony was too complete to ignore: her mother, who had grown ten thousand flowers, now received them in cellophane from the Kroger on Fifth Street, and gazed at them with the polite interest of a stranger.

Today Laura brought daffodils, because it was March, and because daffodils had been the subject of their last real fight.

It had been four years ago, before the diagnosis, before the slow unraveling that the doctors called frontotemporal dementia and that Laura experienced as watching her mother be erased, word by word, memory by memory, like a book left out in the rain. They had argued about the garden. Laura wanted to hire someone to help with the maintenance, because June was seventy-three and the garden was half an acre and Laura had noticed the weeds creeping in, the beds going untended. June had taken this as an accusation of incompetence. The argument escalated in the way arguments between mothers and daughters do, each sentence a door opening onto older rooms, until Laura said something she had not planned to say.

"You care more about that garden than you ever cared about me."

It was not true. It was the kind of thing you say because it will land, because you know exactly where the soft tissue is. June had looked at her with an expression Laura would spend years trying to forget: not anger, but hurt so pure it had no defense against it.

"You know that's not true," June had said quietly.

"Do I?" Laura had replied, and left.

They had not spoken for three weeks after that. When they resumed contact, it was through the careful, cordial exchanges of people who have agreed to pretend that nothing happened. They never discussed the fight. Laura planned to apologize properly at some unspecified future point, when the time was right, when the words came easily, when it wouldn't feel like reopening a wound.

Then the forgetting began.

First the small things: misplaced keys, repeated questions, the names of plants she had known for fifty years dissolving like sugar in water. Then the larger things: how to drive, how to cook, what year it was. Then the people. The neighbors went first. Then old friends. Then Laura's father, dead for a decade but newly dead each time June was reminded. Then, on a Sunday in January, Laura walked into the room and her mother looked at her with courteous blankness and said, "Are you the new aide?"

Laura sat down in the visitor's chair and said, "I'm Laura. I'm your daughter."

June smiled the accommodating smile of someone who does not want to cause trouble. "That's nice," she said.

Now Laura set the daffodils on the bedside table and sat in the chair she always sat in. June looked at the flowers.

"Those are pretty," she said. "What are they called?"

"Daffodils, Mom."

"Daffodils," June repeated, tasting the word as if for the first time.

Laura wanted to say: You grew these. You had a whole bed of them along the south wall of the garden, the ones you called your 'trumpets.' You could tell a King Alfred from a Carlton at twenty paces. You told me once that daffodils were proof that the world wanted to be beautiful.

She wanted to say: I'm sorry about what I said. About the garden and about you not caring. I was wrong, and I knew I was wrong when I said it, and I was going to apologize, I was always going to apologize, I just didn't do it soon enough.

But her mother was gone. Not dead, but gone in a way that was worse than death because the body remained, sitting by the window in a cardigan Laura had buttoned that morning, smiling at flowers she could no longer name.

The apology had no destination. It existed in Laura like an undelivered letter, perfectly composed, permanently unsendable. Her mother would accept it with the same pleasant confusion she accepted everything now, and it would mean nothing, because meaning requires memory, and memory was the first thing the disease had taken.

"Would you like some water, Mom?"

"That would be lovely, dear."

Laura poured water from the plastic pitcher. Her mother drank it and looked out the window at the parking lot, where nothing grew.

The visit lasted an hour, as it always did. Laura talked about her week. June listened with the attentive vacancy of someone at a lecture in a language she doesn't speak. When Laura stood to leave, June looked up.

"Will you come back?" she asked.

"I come every Sunday, Mom."

"That's nice," June said. "I'll look forward to it."

She would not remember this promise. She would not remember Laura's visit. She would not remember the daffodils or the water or the hour they spent together. Next Sunday, Laura would arrive and introduce herself again, and her mother would smile the accommodating smile, and the apology would remain where it was, locked inside Laura, undeliverable, permanent.

Laura drove home through streets that were beginning to bloom with spring. On every corner, daffodils were pushing through the last of the winter soil, yellow and insistent, trumpeting to no one.

David taught his son to swim in the summer of 2011, in the lake behind the cabin they rented every August. James was five and afraid of the water, the way children are afraid of things that are bigger than they are, which is to say afraid of almost everything.

David was patient in the water in a way he was not patient on land. On land, he was a man who checked his phone during conversations and forgot school pickup days and worked late without calling. But in the lake, with his son's small hands gripping his forearms, he was entirely present. He made it a game. He invented creatures that lived in the shallows, friendly ones with ridiculous names. He held James up with one hand under his belly while James kicked and splashed and laughed.

"Kick harder," David would say. "That's it. I've got you. I've got you."

And then the moment every parent lives for: he let go, and James swam. Three strokes, then four, then six, his small body cutting through the water with the graceless determination of someone discovering they can do something they thought they couldn't. James surfaced, gasping, grinning, looking for his father's face.

"Dad! Dad, did you see?"

"I saw. I saw everything."

They went back to the cabin and David grilled hot dogs and they sat on the porch watching the lake go dark. James fell asleep on the couch with his hair still damp. David carried him to bed and thought, with the uncomplicated certainty of a good day, that this was what mattered. This. Everything else was noise.

He did not know, on that August evening, that he was making a memory he would one day be unable to survive.

The flood came in the spring of 2024. James was eighteen, home from his first year of college, helping with the sandbagging effort along the river that ran through their town. The news called it a five-hundred-year event, which meant nothing to the water, which did not count years. The levee broke at 4:15 a.m. The water came not as a wave but as a rising, the way water always comes, filling what was empty, covering what was low.

David was fifty miles away, at a conference he had almost cancelled. He watched the news on his phone in a hotel room in a city that was dry and bright and impossible. The coverage was aerial, helicopter shots of brown water where streets had been, of rooftops, of rescue boats. The reporter's voice was steady in the way that trained voices are steady during catastrophe.

He called James. No answer. He called his ex-wife, who had James's location on her phone. The dot was stationary, in the neighborhood near the river, the neighborhood where the levee had broken.

He drove the fifty miles in thirty-seven minutes. The road was blocked four miles from town. He parked and walked and then ran, through mud, past National Guard trucks, past families carrying what they could carry, past the smell of river water, which is the smell of the earth's interior made suddenly exterior.

They found James's body two days later, in a field a mile from the river. He had been helping an elderly neighbor evacuate when the water rose too fast. The neighbor survived. She told David, later, that James had pushed her toward a tree and told her to hold on. That he had gone back for her cat. That the last thing she saw was his head above the water, swimming, swimming hard, the way someone swims when they know how.

David listened to the neighbor and nodded and thanked her and drove to the cabin by the lake, which was untouched by the flood because it was on high ground, because geography is not interested in symmetry. He walked to the water's edge and stood where he had stood thirteen years ago, one hand extended over the surface, as if he could still feel the weight of a small body learning to float.

He had taught his son to swim. His son had died in water.

This was the fact that would not resolve, the equation that would not balance. He had given James the skill and the confidence and the courage to enter the water, and the water had taken him. The same water, the same swimming, the same letting go.

David stood at the lake until the light failed. He did not swim. He would never swim again. The lake was the same lake it had always been, clear and cold and indifferent, and somewhere in its depth, or in the depth of every body of water he would ever see again, was the sound of a five-year-old boy surfacing, gasping, grinning, calling out: Dad. Dad, did you see?

The morning Omar left, the sky over Aleppo was the impossible blue that comes after rain, the kind of blue that makes you believe the world is clean. He remembers this because he has spent every day since trying to decide if the beauty of that sky was a cruelty or a mercy.
